Question:
Statement I: When certain indigenous species are introduced into a geographical area, they become invasive and start spreading fast because the invaded land does not have its natural predators
Statement II: Nearly 75% of all insects are known to be phytophagous (feeding on plant sap and other parts of plants)
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: When certain exotic species are introduced into a geographical area, they become invasive and start spreading fast because the invaded land does not have its natural predators
Statement II is incorrect: Nearly 25% of all insects are known to be phytophagous (feeding on plant sap and other parts of plants)

Question:
Statement I: Tropical Amazonian rain forest in South America has the greatest biodiversity on Earth
Statement II: Tropics harbour more species than temperate or polar areas because tropical environments are more seasonal and less constant
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: Tropical environments are less seasonal, relatively more constant, and predictable. It is this stability that promotes niche specialization and higher species diversity.

Question:
Statement I: According to Hugo de Vries, it is mutation that causes speciation and hence he called it saltation (single-step large mutation)
Statement II: Darwinian variations are random and directionless, while mutations are small and directional
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: Darwinian variations are small and directional, while mutations are random and directionless.

Question:
Read the following statements regarding plant anatomy and choose the correct sequence of True (T) and False (F):
Statement I: Monocot roots have epidermis, cortex, endodermis, pericycle, vascular bundles and pith
Statement II: In Dicot stems, the pericycle is present on the inner side of the endodermis and above the phloem in the form of semilunar patches of collenchyma
Statement III: Reticulate venation is characteristic of isobilateral leaves, while parallel venation is characteristic of dorsiventral leaves
Statement IV: Mesophyll in leaves, which possesses chloroplasts and carry out photosynthesis, is made up of parenchyma
Options: I-T, II-F, III-F, IV-T
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-T
I-F, II-F, III-T, IV-T
I-F, II-T, III-F, IV-T
Solution:
Statement II is false: In Dicot stems, the pericycle is present on the inner side of the endodermis and above the phloem in the form of semilunar patches of sclerenchyma not collenchyma
Statement III is false: Venation is swapped. Reticulate is for Dorsiventral (Dicot) and Parallel is for Isobilateral (Monocot)

Question:
Assertion (A): Secretions of the bulbourethral glands constitute the major part of seminal plasma
Reason (R): Seminal plasma is rich in fructose, calcium, and certain enzymes
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is false: The major portion of seminal plasma is contributed by the seminal vesicles and the prostate gland, not the bulbourethral glands.

Question:
Assertion (A): The occurrence of more than one embryo in a seed is referred to as polyembryony
Reason (R): In many Citrus and Mango varieties, some of the nucellar cells surrounding the embryo sac start dividing and protrude into the embryo sac to develop into embryos
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The protrusion of nucellar cells into the embryo sac (Reason) creates multiple embryos within a single ovule, which is the direct cause of the state known as polyembryony

Question:
Select the correct set of answer among the following:
Statement I: The isolated lysosomal vesicles have been found to be very rich in almost all types of hydrolytic enzymes optimally active at the alkaline pH
Statement II: The number of mitochondria per cell is variable depending on the physiological activity of the cells
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Solution: Statement I is Incorrect: The isolated lysosomal vesicles have been found to be very rich in almost all types of hydrolytic enzymes optimally active at the acidic pH

Question:
Statement I: Cells in the quiescent stage (G0) remain metabolically active but no longer proliferate unless called on to do so.
Statement II: Cells enter the G0 phase by exiting the cell cycle at the end of the G2 phase.
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: Cells exit the cell cycle at the end of the G1 phase (not G2) to enter G0.

Question:
Assertion (A): Reptilia refers to the creeping or crawling mode of locomotion found in the animals belonging to this class
Reason (R): Reptiles generally possess a three-chambered heart with two ventricles and one partially divided auricle
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Reason (R) is false: Reptiles generally possess a three-chambered heart with two auricles and one partially divided ventricle

Question:
Select the correct statements:
Statement I: Substances responsible for occupational respiratory disorders are often met within industries involving stone breaking or grinding
Statement II: The respiratory rhythm center which regulates respiration is located in the cerebrum
Statement III: CO2 is carried by haemoglobin as carbamino-haemoglobin (about 70%)
Statement IV: An increase in H+ concentration in the body will activate the chemosensitive area to signal the rhythm center to make necessary adjustments
Options: I and II only
II and III only
I and IV only
III and IV only
Solution: Statement III is Incorrect: Carbamino-haemoglobin accounts for 20-25%. 70% is Bicarbonate.

Question:
Statement I: Human beings have an insignificant ability to maintain and moderate the respiratory rhythm to suit the demands of the body tissues
Statement II: This is done by the endocrine system
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution:
Statement I is Incorrect: Human beings have a significant ability to maintain and moderate the respiratory rhythm to suit the demands of the body tissues
Statement II is Incorrect: This is done by the neural system

Question:
Statement I: Skeletal muscles are closely associated with the skeletal components of the body and have a striped appearance
Statement II: As their activities are under the voluntary control of the nervous system, they are known as involuntary muscles
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: Since they are under voluntary control, they are known as voluntary muscles (not involuntary)

Column I

 

Column II

Labrum

Lower lip

B.

Mandible

Upper lip

Hypopharynx

Labium

Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-iii, C-iv, D-i

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: During fertilisation, a sperm comes in contact with the zona pellucida layer of the ovum and induces changes in the membrane that block the entry of additional sperms
Statement II: The secretions of the acrosome help the sperm enter into the cytoplasm of the ovum through the corona radiata and the plasma membrane
Statement III: The second meiotic division is completed by the secondary oocyte only after the entry of the sperm
Statement IV: The second meiotic division is an equal division resulting in the formation of a haploid ootid and a second polar body
Options: Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements I and III are correct
Statements II, III and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement II is incorrect: The secretions of the acrosome help the sperm enter into the cytoplasm of the ovum through the zona pellucida and the plasma membrane
Statement IV is incorrect: The second meiotic division, like the first, is an unequal division. It produces a large haploid ootid (ovum) and a tiny second polar body.

Question:
State whether the following statements are True (T) or False (F):
Statement I: We have the ability to increase the strength of inspiration and expiration with the help of additional muscles in the abdomen
Statement II: On average, a healthy human breathes 12-16 times/minute
Statement III: A spirometer helps in the clinical assessment of pulmonary functions but cannot estimate the Residual Volume
Statement IV: Expiration occurs when the intra-pulmonary pressure is lower than the atmospheric pressure
Options: I-T, II-F, III-F, IV-F
I-T, II-T, III-T, IV-F
I-F, II-F, III-T, IV-T
I-F, II-T, III-F, IV-T
Solution: Statement IV is False: Expiration takes place when the intra-pulmonary pressure is higher than the atmospheric pressure

Question:
Assertion (A): During prophase, each centrosome radiates out microtubules called asters
Reason (R): The two asters together with the spindle fibres form the mitotic apparatus
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
Solution: Both statements are factually true. However, R is a definition of a larger structure and does not explain why the centrosome radiates microtubules (which is due to microtubule nucleation properties). Thus, it is not the correct explanation.

Question:
Assertion (A): Insulin is a polymer of fructose
Reason (R): Polysaccharides are long chains of sugars containing different monosaccharides as building blocks
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is False: Inulin not insulin is a polymer of fructose

Question:
Statement I: Some mature cells like erythrocytes of many mammals and sieve tube cells of vascular plants even lack nucleus
Statement II: In acrocentric chromosomes, the centromere is situated close to its end, forming one extremely short and one very long arm
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.
Solution:
Statement I is Correct: Some mature cells even lack nucleus, e.g., erythrocytes of many mammals and sieve tube cells of vascular plants
Statement II is Correct: Acrocentric chromosomes have the centromere situated close to its end forming one extremely short and one very long arm (J shaped)

Question:
Read the following statements carefully regarding Solanaceae and select the correct options among the following:
Statement I: This family is commonly called the 'legume family' and is widely distributed in tropics, subtropics and even temperate zones
Statement II: The stem is rarely woody, aerial, erect, cylindrical, branched, solid or hollow, and hairy or glabrous
Statement III: The fruits are typically berries or capsules and the seeds are many and non-endospermous
Statement IV: The gynoecium is bicarpellary obligately placed, syncarpous, ovary superior, bilocular, and placenta swollen with many ovules
Options: Statements I, III and IV are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Statements I, II and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: This family is commonly called the 'potato family' and is widely distributed in tropics, subtropics and even temperate zones
Statement III is incorrect: The fruits are typically berries or capsules and the seeds are many and endospermous

Question:
Assertion (A): In lactic acid fermentation, there is a vigorous release of CO2
Reason (R): In animal muscle cells during exercise, when oxygen is inadequate, pyruvic acid is reduced to lactic acid by lactate dehydrogenase
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is False: Lactic acid fermentation (unlike alcoholic fermentation) does not produce CO2. It converts Pyruvic acid C3 directly to Lactic acid (C3).

Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A

A-ii, B-i, C-iii, D-iv
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-iii, C-i, D-iv

Tags.Subject: Zoology
Tags.Topic: Tools of Recombinant DNA Technology - Cloning Vectors

Question:
Assertion (A): DNA evolved from RNA with chemical modifications
Reason (R): Double-stranded nature and the presence of thymine are modifications in DNA that provide resistance to change
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: RNA was the first genetic material (RNA World). However, due to its reactivity, it was not ideal for long-term storage. DNA evolved as a more stable version by becoming double-stranded (allowing for repair mechanisms) and replacing Uracil with Thymine, which makes the molecule more resistant to mutations.

Question:
Consider the following statements:
Statement I: Nearly 70 percent of CO2 is transported by red blood cells as carbamino-haemoglobin
Statement II: The respiratory rhythm centre is located in the pons region of the brain and is primarily responsible for regulation
Statement III: Asthma is a difficulty in breathing causing wheezing due to inflammation of bronchi and bronchioles
Statement IV: The role of oxygen in the regulation of respiratory rhythm is quite insignificant
Which of the above statements are CORRECT?
Options: I, II and III
I, III and IV
III and IV only
All of the above
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: Nearly 20-25 percent of CO2 is transported by red blood cells as carbamino-haemoglobin
Statement II is Incorrect: The respiratory rhythm centre is located in the medulla region, not pons.

Question:
Statement I: The endosperm in Angiosperms is formed after fertilization and is a triploid tissue
Statement II: The endosperm in Gymnosperms is formed before fertilization and is a haploid tissue
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct

Question: Match the following and choose the correct option:
Solution: The correct matching is: A-4, B-3, C-2, D-1

Question:
Match the Phase in Column I with the key physiological event in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Menstrual Phase(i)Regeneration of endometrium through proliferation
B.Follicular Phase(ii)Breakdown of endometrial lining and blood vessels
C.Luteal Phase(iii)Peak of LH and FSH inducing follicle rupture
D.Ovulatory Phase(iv)Secretion of progesterone to maintain endometrium
Options: A-i, B-ii, C-iii, D-iv
A-iv, B-iii, C-ii, D-i
A-ii, B-i, C-iv, D-iii
A-ii, B-iv, C-i, D-iii
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-i, C-iv, D-iii

Question:
Statement I: The ovaries are located one on each side of the upper abdomen
Statement II: Each ovary is about 2 to 4 cm in length and is connected to the pelvic wall and uterus by ligaments
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is incorrect: The ovaries are located one on each side of the lower abdomen

Question:
Statement I: RNA stands for Ribo-Nucleic Acid and contains the sugar ribulose
Statement II: The heterocyclic ring skeleton of Adenine is called the Pyrimidine ring
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: RNA contains ribose, not ribulose
Statement II is incorrect: Adenine is a purine, not pyrimidine. It is a two ringed structure- the imidazole ring and the pyrimidine ring

Question:
Match the Phenomenon in Column I with its biological Context in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Polyembryony(i)Development of fruit without fertilisation (e.g., Banana)
B.Apomixis(ii)Mimics sexual reproduction; seeds without fertilisation
C.Parthenocarpy(iii)Nucellar cells divide and protrude into embryo sac
D.Dormancy(iv)State of inactivity and reduced metabolic rate in seeds
Options: A-iii, B-i, C-iv, D-ii
A-i, B-ii, C-iii, D-iv
A-iv, B-iii, C-ii, D-i
A-ii, B-iii, C-i, D-iv
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-iii, C-i, D-iv

Question:
Statement I: The canal called the cerebral aqueduct passes through the forebrain
Statement II: The dorsal portion of the midbrain consists mainly of four round swellings (lobes) called corpora quadrigemina
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is Incorrect: The cerebral aqueduct passes through the midbrain, not the forebrain.

Question:
Read the following statements and choose the correct sequence of True (T) and False (F):
Statement I: In amphibians, respiration is by gills, lungs and through skin
Statement II: Crocodiles possess a three-chambered heart
Statement III: In birds, the bones are pneumatic (hollow with air cavities)
Statement IV: Macropus is an example of an oviparous mammal
Options: I-F, II-F, III-T, IV-T
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-F
I-T, II-F, III-T, IV-F
I-F, II-T, III-F, IV-T
Solution:
Statement II is False: Crocodiles are the only reptiles with a four-chambered heart.
Statement IV is False: Macropus (Kangaroo) is viviparous (specifically a marsupial). The oviparous mammal is the Platypus.

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: In human beings, after one month of pregnancy, the embryo’s heart is formed.
Statement II: Immediately after implantation, the inner cell mass (embryo) differentiates into an outer layer called endoderm and an inner layer called ectoderm
Statement III: Two germ layers – ectoderm and endoderm give rise to all tissues (organs) in human adults
Statement IV: Breast-feeding during the initial period of infant growth is recommended by doctors for bringing up a healthy baby
Options: Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements I and III are correct
Statements II and III are not incorrect
Statements I and IV are not incorrect
Solution:
Statement II is incorrect: Immediately after implantation, the inner cell mass (embryo) differentiates into an outer layer called ectoderm and an inner layer called endoderm
Statement III: Three germ layers- ectoderm, endoderm and mesoderm give rise to all tissues (organs) in adults

Question:
Read the following statements regarding respiratory organs in different animals:
Statement I: Sponges, coelenterates, and flatworms exchange O2 with CO2 by simple diffusion over their entire body surface
Statement II: Earthworms use their moist cuticle for respiration, while insects have a network of spiracle tubes
Statement III: Aquatic arthropods and molluscs respire through vascularised lungs
Statement IV: Among vertebrates, fishes use gills, whereas reptiles, birds, and mammals respire through lungs
Which of the above statements are correct?
Options: II and IV only
II and III only
I and IV only
All of the above
Solution:
Statement II: Earthworms use their moist cuticle for respiration, while insects have a network of tracheal tubes
Statement III is Incorrect: Aquatic arthropods and molluscs respire through special vascularised structures called gills (branchial respiration), not lungs. Lungs are characteristic of terrestrial forms.

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct statements among the following:
Statement I: In animal cells, cytokinesis is achieved by the appearance of a furrow in the plasma membrane
Statement II: In plant cells, wall formation is centripetal i.e. it starts in the centre of the cell and grows outward to meet the existing lateral walls
Statement III: The formation of the new cell wall in plants begins with the formation of a simple precursor, called the cell plate
Statement IV: The cell plate represents the middle lamella between the walls of two adjacent cells
Options: Only I and II
Only II and III
Only I, III, and IV
All of the above (I, II, III, IV)
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: In plant cells, wall formation is centrifugal i.e. it starts in the centre of the cell and grows outward to meet the existing lateral walls.

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct statements among the following:
Statement I: Telophase is effectively the reverse of prophase, where chromosomes lose their individuality and collect in a mass at the two poles
Statement II: The nuclear envelope assembles around the chromosome clusters during prophase
Statement III: Mitosis usually results in the production of diploid daughter cells with identical genetic complement
Statement IV: The growth of multicellular organisms is due to mitosis, but it plays no role in cell repair
Options: I, II, and III are correct
I and III are correct
II and III are correct
I, II, III, and IV are correct
Solution:
Statement II is Incorrect: The nuclear envelope assembles around the chromosome clusters during telophase.
Statement IV is Incorrect: Mitosis is significant for cell repair (e.g., cells of the upper layer of the epidermis, cells of the lining of the gut, and blood cells are being constantly replaced).

Question:
Statement I: Internal structures do not show adaptations to diverse environments
Statement II: Gymnosperms lack sieve tubes and companion cells; instead, they possess sieve cells and albuminous cells
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is incorrect: Internal structures also show adaptations to diverse environments

Question:
Statement I: The pineal gland secretes a hormone called melanin which regulates the skin colour
Statement II: The hormone secreted by the pineal gland influences metabolism, pigmentation, the menstrual cycle, and our defense capability
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is Incorrect: The pineal gland secretes Melatonin, not melanin. (Melanin is a pigment, Melatonin is a hormone)

Question:
Statement I: Dicotyledonous stem is covered with a thin layer of cuticle and it may bear trichomes and many stomata
Statement II: In dicot roots, the cells arranged in multiple layers between epidermis and pericycle constitute the cortex which consists of three sub-zones
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: Dicotyledonous stem is covered with a thin layer of cuticle and it may bear trichomes and a few not many stomata
Statement II is incorrect: In dicot stems not roots, the cells arranged in multiple layers between epidermis and pericycle constitute the cortex which consists of three sub-zones

Question:
Read the statements given below and choose the correct option
Statement I: Numerical Taxonomy is now easily carried out using computers is based on all observable characteristics
Statement II: In Numerical Taxonomy, number and codes are assigned to all the characters and the data are then processed
Statement III: Cytotaxonomy is based on chemical constituents of the plant to resolve confusions
Statement IV: Chemotaxonomy is based on cytological information like chromosome number, structure and behaviour
Options: Statements I and II are correct
Statements II and III are correct
Statements I, II and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement III is incorrect: Cytotaxonomy is based on cytological information (chromosomes), not chemical constituents.
Statement IV is incorrect: Chemotaxonomy is based on chemical constituents, not cytological info.

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer among the following:
Statement I: Artificial systems of classification, such as the one given by Linnaeus, were based on androecium structure and other reproductive characters
Statement II: Artificial systems gave equal weightage to vegetative and sexual characteristics; this was not acceptable since vegetative characters are more easily affected by the environment
Statement III: Natural classification systems, like the one proposed by George Bentham and Joseph Dalton Hooker, considered external features as well as internal features like ultrastructure, anatomy, and embryology
Statement IV: Phylogenetic classification systems assume that organisms belonging to the same taxa have a common ancestor
Options: Statements I and III are correct
Statements II, III and IV are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement I is incorrect: Artificial systems of classification, such as the one given by Linnaeus, were based on androecium structure and vegetative characters not other reproductive characters.

Question:
Match the Feature in Column I with its Evolutionary implication/Context in Column II:

 

Column I

 

Column II

A

First terrestrial vascular plants

(i)

Precursor to the seed habit

B

Retention of female gametophyte

(ii)

Restricted geographical spread

C

Water requirement for fertilization

(iii)

Possess xylem and phloem

D

Zygote development inside gametophyte

(iv)

Occurs in heterosporous species
Options: A-iii, B-i, C-ii, D-iv
A-ii, B-iv, C-i, D-iii
A-iii, B-iv, C-ii, D-i
A-i, B-iii, C-ii, D-iv
Solution: The correct matching is: A-iii, B-iv, C-ii, D-i

Question:
Read the following four statements regarding how hormones exert their effects and identify the correct sequence of True (T) or False (F):
Statement I: Hormones like insulin and glucagon interact with membrane-bound receptors and generally do not enter the target cell
Statement II: Steroid hormones and iodothyronines mostly regulate gene expression or chromosome function by the interaction of the hormone-receptor complex with the genome
Statement III: Second messengers like cyclic AMP, IP3, and Ca++ are typically generated by hormones that interact with intracellular receptors
Statement IV: Physiological responses produced by hormones acting through intracellular receptors are usually slower but long-lasting compared to those acting through membrane-bound receptors
Select the correct option:
Options: I-T, II-F, III-F, IV-F
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-T
I-F, II-F, III-T, IV-T
I-F, II-T, III-F, IV-T
Solution: Statement III is False: Second messengers are generated by hormones interacting with membrane-bound receptors (since the hormone itself cannot enter). Intracellular receptor hormones usually act directly on the genome without generating second messengers like cAMP.

Question:
Assertion (A): CT scans are considered safer than MRI for repeated diagnostic use in cancer patients
Reason (R): MRI uses strong magnetic fields and non-ionizing radiations to detect pathological changes
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is false: MRI is actually considered safer for repeated use because it uses non-ionizing radiation (magnetic fields and radio waves), whereas CT (Computed Tomography) uses X-rays, which are ionizing radiation and carry a cumulative risk of DNA damage.

Question:
Statement I: Regulation of the lac operon by the repressor is referred to as negative regulation
Statement II: The lac operon can be switched on only in the presence of an inducer like lactose or allolactose
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct

Question:
Match the Respiratory Volume in Column I with its approximate value in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Tidal Volume (TV)(1)2500 – 3000 ml
B.Inspiratory Reserve Volume (IRV)(2)1000 – 1100 ml
C.Expiratory Reserve Volume (ERV)(3)500 ml
D.Residual Volume (RV)(4)1100 – 1200 ml
Solution: The correct matching is: A-3, B-1, C-2, D-4

Question:
Assertion (A): As we go higher from species to kingdom, the number of common characteristics goes on decreasing
Reason (R): Lower the taxa, more are the characteristics that the members within the taxon share
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The reason correctly explains the assertion. The fundamental concept of the hierarchy is that specificity decreases as rank increases; therefore, the decrease in common characteristics at higher levels is a direct result of the higher specificity (more shared traits) at lower levels.

Question:
Statement I: Parathyroid hormone (PTH) acts on bones and stimulates the process of bone resorption
Statement II: PTH stimulates the excretion of calcium by the renal tubules, thereby decreasing blood calcium levels
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: PTH stimulates reabsorption (not excretion) of Ca2+ by the renal tubules and increases blood calcium levels (it is a hypercalcaemic hormone).

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: Bacteria are grouped under four categories based on their shape
Statement II: The spherical bacteria are called Coccus (pl.: Cocci).
Statement III: The rod-shaped bacteria are called Bacillus (pl.: Bacilli).
Statement IV: The comma-shaped bacteria are called Spirillum (pl.: Spirilla) and the spiral ones are called Vibrio (pl.: Vibrio).
Options: Statements II and IV are correct
Statements I, III and IV are correct
Statements I, II and III are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: The comma-shaped bacteria are called Vibrio (pl.: Vibrio), and the spiral bacteria are called Spirillum (pl.: Spirilla).

Question:
Statement I: The systemic circulation begins with the pumping of oxygenated blood by the left ventricle into the aorta
Statement II: The pulmonary circulation begins with the pumping of deoxygenated blood by the left atrium into the pulmonary artery
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: Pulmonary circulation starts at the Right Ventricle, which pumps deoxygenated blood into the pulmonary artery. The left atrium receives oxygenated blood.

Question:
State whether the following statements are True (T) or False (F):
A. As the filtrate moves down the descending limb of the loop of Henle, it becomes hypertonic
B. The ascending limb of the loop of Henle is impermeable to water
C. As the concentrated filtrate passes upward in the ascending limb, it gets diluted due to the passage of electrolytes to the medullary fluid
D. DCT is incapable of reabsorbing HCO3-
Options: I-T, II-F, III-F, IV-F
I-F, II-F, III-T, IV-T
I-T, II-T, III-T, IV-F
I-F, II-T, III-F, IV-T
Solution: False: DCT is capable of reabsorption of HCO3-

Question: Identify A to F in the given diagram:
Options: A–Pollen tube, B–Ovary, C–Ovule, D–Antipodal cell, E–Pollen grain, F–Polar nuclei
A–Polar nuclei, B–Antipodal cell, C–Ovule, D–Ovary, E–Pollen tube, F–Pollen grain
A–Pollen grain, B–Pollen tube, C–Ovary, D–Ovule, E–Antipodal cell, F– Polar nuclei
A–Antipodal cell, B–Ovule, C–Ovary, D–Polar nuclei, E–Pollen grain, F–Pollen tube
Solution: The correct matching is: A–Pollen grain, B–Pollen tube, C–Ovary, D–Ovule, E–Antipodal cell, F– Polar nuclei

Question:

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct statements:
Statement I: The tongue of a frog is bilobed at the tip and is used to capture prey
Statement II: Digestion involves the action of HCl and gastric juices secreted from the walls of the stomach
Statement III: A special venous connection between the liver and intestine is called the renal portal system
Statement IV: The blood consists of plasma and cells; the erythrocytes (RBCs) are oval, biconvex, and enucleated
Identify the statement/(s) that are not incorrect:
Options: Only Statement II
Only Statement III
Statements III and IV
Statements I and II
Solution:
Statement III is incorrect: The special venous connection between the liver and intestine is called the Hepatic Portal System. The Renal Portal System connects the kidneys and lower body
Statement IV is incorrect: The blood consists of plasma and cells; the erythrocytes (RBCs) are oval, biconvex, and nucleated

Question:
Statement I: Neutrophils, eosinophils, and basophils are different types of granulocytes
Statement II: Lymphocytes and monocytes are the agranulocytes
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct

Question:
Assertion (A): In some organisms, a multinucleate condition known as syncytium is formed
Reason (R): In these cases, karyokinesis is not followed by cytokinesis
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are true and R provides the direct cause for the condition described in A..

Question:
Match Column I (Structure) with Column II (Function):
Column IColumn II
A.Microvilli(1)Cementing to keep neighbouring cells together
B.Tight Junctions(2)Facilitate communication by connecting cytoplasm
C.Adhering Junctions(3)Increase surface area for absorption
D.Gap Junctions(4)Stop substances from leaking across a tissue
Solution: The correct matching is: A-3, B-4, C-1, D-2

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: Cyanobacteria, also referred to as blue-green algae, have chlorophyll 'a' similar to green plants and are photosynthetic autotrophs
Statement II: They are unicellular, colonial, or filamentous, freshwater, marine, or terrestrial algae
Statement III: The colonies are generally surrounded by a gelatinous sheath and they often form blooms in polluted water bodies
Statement IV: Some of these organisms can fix atmospheric nitrogen in specialised cells called nodules, e.g. Nostoc and Anabaena
Options: Statements I, II and IV are correct
Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements I, III and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: Some of these organisms can fix atmospheric nitrogen in specialised cells called heterocysts not nodules, e.g. Nostoc and Anabaena

Question:
Assertion (A): The dominant phase in the life cycle of a Pteridophyte is the gametophyte
Reason (R): The prothallus is an inconspicuous, small but multicellular, free-living, mostly photosynthetic thalloid gametophyte
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is Incorrect: The dominant phase in Pteridophytes is the Sporophyte, not the gametophyte.

Question:
Read the following statements and choose the correct sequence of True (T) and False (F):
Statement I: Primers are primers small chemically synthesised mononucleotides that are complementary to the regions of DNA
Statement II: Availability of synthetic primers contributed popularising the PCR technique
Statement III: A sparged stirred-tank bioreactor has a stirrer but lacks the mechanism to increase the surface area for oxygen transfer
Statement IV: A continuous culture system should be chosen for best yield if one were to produce a recombinant protein in large amounts
Options: I-T, II-F, III-F, IV-F
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-T
I-F, II-F, III-F, IV-T
I-F, II-T, III-F, IV-T
Solution:
Statement I is false: Primers are primers small chemically synthesised oligonucleotides that are complementary to the regions of DNA
Statement II is false: Availability of ‘thermostable’ DNA polymerase contributed popularising the PCR technique
Statement III is False: In a sparged stirred-tank bioreactor, sterile air is bubbled through the system, which actually increases the surface area for oxygen transfer.

Tags.Subject: Botany
Tags.Chapter: Strategies for enhancement in food production

Question:
Statement I: Entamoeba histolytica is a protozoan parasite in the large intestine of human which causes amoebiasis (amoebic dysentery)
Statement II: Houseflies act as mechanical carriers and serve to transmit the parasite from faeces of infected person to food and food products, thereby contaminating them
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct

Question:
Select the correct answer after studying the following statements:
Statement I: Adolescence is a period between 12-18 years marked by biological and behavioural changes
Statement II: Smoking increases carbon dioxide (CO2) content in blood and reduces the concentration of haembound oxygen
Statement III: If a pregnant woman uses drugs or alcohol, it is known to adversely affect the foetus
Statement IV: Misuse of anabolic steroids in females causes decreased aggressiveness, mood swings, and lengthening of the menstrual cycle
Options: Statements I, II, and III are correct
Statements I and IV are correct
Statements I and III are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement II is incorrect: Smoking increases carbon monoxide (CO) content in blood and reduces the concentration of haembound oxygen
Statement IV is incorrect: The use of anabolic steroids in females actually causes increased aggressiveness and abnormal (irregular) menstrual cycles, along with masculinization.

Question:
Statement I: Double fertilisation involves two types of nuclear fusions, syngamy and triple fusion, occurring in the same embryo sac
Statement II: The central cell, after triple fusion, becomes the Primary Endosperm Cell (PEC) and develops into the endosperm.
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct

Question:
Statement I: Work of Thomas Malthus on populations influenced Darwin
Statement II: Hugo DeVries based on his work on snapdragon brought forth the idea of mutations – large difference arising suddenly in a population
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: Hugo DeVries based on his work on evening primrose brought forth the idea of mutations – large difference arising suddenly in a population

Question: Match the cause in Column I with the specific example in Column II:
Options: A-iii, B-i, C-ii, D-iv
A-i, B-ii, C-iii, D-iv
A-iv, B-iii, C-ii, D-i
A-ii, B-iv, C-i, D-iii
Solution: The correct matching is: A-iii, B-i, C-ii, D-iv

Question:
Statement I: According to the chemiosmotic hypothesis, the breakdown of the proton gradient across the thylakoid membrane provides the energy to cause a conformational change in the F1 particle of the ATP synthase
Statement II: The primary acceptor of electrons in the non-cyclic photo-phosphorylation transfers its electron not to an electron carrier but to a hydrogen carrier (proton pump)
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution:
Statement I is correct: The breakdown of the gradient releases energy, which activates the ATP synthase enzyme (F1 part) to synthesise ATP
Statement II is correct: The primary acceptor transfers the electron to a H-carrier (Plastoquinone), which removes a proton from the stroma while transporting an electron

Question:
Select the correct answer after studying the following statements:
Statement I: Geitonogamy is the transfer of pollen grains from the anther to the stigma of another flower of the same plant
Statement II: Functional geitonogamy is a type of autogamy since the pollen grains come from the same plant
Statement III: Genetically, geitonogamy is similar to cross-pollination involving a pollinating agent
Statement IV: Xenogamy is the only type of pollination which during pollination brings genetically different types of pollen grains to the stigma
Options: Statements I and IV are correct
Statements I, III and IV are correct
Statements II, III and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement II: Functional geitonogamy is a type of cross-pollination involving a pollinating agent
Statement III: Genetically, geitonogamy is similar to autogamy since the pollen grains come from the same plant

Question:
Statement I: The thyroid gland secretes a protein hormone called Thyrocalcitonin (TCT)
Statement II: TCT regulates the blood calcium levels by opposing the action of Parathyroid Hormone (PTH)
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct

Question:
Read the following four statements:
Statement I: In the chloroplasts, the membrane system is responsible for trapping light energy and synthesizing ATP and NADPH
Statement II: The stroma contains enzymes required for the synthesis of sugar, which in turn leads to the formation of starch
Statement III: Leaf pigments of any green plant can be separated through paper chromatography, showing that the colour we see is due to a single pigment, Chlorophyll a
Statement IV: Accessory pigments like chlorophyll b, xanthophylls, and carotenoids absorb light and transfer the energy to chlorophyll a, protecting it from photo-oxidation
Which of the following combinations of statements is correct?
Options: Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Statements II and III are correct
Statements I, III, and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement III is Incorrect: Paper chromatography reveals that the colour of leaves is not due to a single pigment but due to four pigments: Chlorophyll a (bright or blue-green), Chlorophyll b (yellow-green), Xanthophylls (yellow), and Carotenoids (yellow to yellow-orange).

Question:
Statement I: In cyclic photophosphorylation, only PS I is functional and the electron circulates within the photosystem
Statement II: Cyclic photophosphorylation results in the synthesis of ATP, NADPH, and H+
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: Cyclic photophosphorylation results only in the synthesis of ATP, but not of NADPH + H+

Question:
Assertion (A): In competitive inhibition, the presence of the inhibitor increases the Km value of the enzyme
Reason (R): Competitive inhibitors bind irreversibly to the active site, destroying the enzyme's catalytic capability forever
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Reason is False: Competitive inhibition is usually reversible. The inhibitor competes for the active site, but the effect can be reversed by increasing the substrate concentration. It does not destroy the enzyme forever (which would be non-competitive or irreversible inhibition).

Question:
Statement I: All eukaryotic cells are identical
Statement II: Gas vacuoles are found in blue green and purple and green photosynthetic bacteria
Options: Both statements I and II are correct
Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct
Both statements I and II are incorrect
Solution: All eukaryotic cells are not identical. Plant and animal cells are different as the former possess cell walls, plastids and a large central vacuole which are absent in animal cells.

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: Mitochondria are the sites of anaerobic respiration
Statement II: ER divides the intercellular space into two distinct compartments, i.e., luminal (inside ER) and extra luminal (cytoplasm) compartments
Statement III: In plant cells, the vacuoles can occupy up to 90% of the volume of the cell
Statement IV: The endomembrane system includes mitochondria, chloroplast and peroxisomes
Options: Statements I, II and IV are incorrect
Statements III and IV are incorrect
Only Statement I is incorrect
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement I is Incorrect: Mitochondria are the sites of aerobic respiration
Statement II is Incorrect: ER divides the intracellular space into two distinct compartments, i.e., luminal (inside ER) and extra luminal (cytoplasm) compartments
Statement IV is Incorrect: The endomembrane system includes endoplasmic reticulum (ER), golgi complex, lysosomes and vacuoles. Since the functions of the mitochondria, chloroplast and peroxisomes are not coordinated with the above components, these are not considered as part of the endomembrane system

Question:
State whether the following statements are True (T) or False (F):
1. Receptors associated with the aortic arch and carotid artery can recognize changes in CO2 and H+ concentration
2. Occupational respiratory disorders like silicosis involve proliferation of fibrous tissue (fibrosis) in the upper lungs.
3. Every 100 ml of deoxygenated blood delivers approximately 4 ml of CO2 to the alveoli
4. Cigarette smoking is a major cause of Asthma
Options: I-T, II-F, III-F, IV-F
I-T, II-T, III-T, IV-F
I-F, II-F, III-T, IV-T
I-F, II-T, III-F, IV-T
Solution: Cigarette smoking is the major cause of Emphysema, not Asthma

Question:
Read the following statements and choose the correct option:
Statement I: The gynoecium is the female reproductive part of the flower and is made up of one or more carpels
Statement II: When more than one carpel is present, they may be free (as in Lotus and Rose) and are called apocarpous
Statement III: When carpels are fused (as in Mustard and Tomato), they are called syncarpous
Statement IV: After fertilization, the ovules develop into fruits and the ovary matures into a seed
Options: Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Statements I and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: After fertilization, the ovules develop into seeds and the ovary matures into a fruit.

Question:
Assertion (A): In monocotyledonous seeds like maize, the embryo is small and situated in a groove at one end of the endosperm
Reason (R): The seed coat is membranous and generally fused with the fruit wall
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are true facts about maize seeds. However, the nature of the seed coat (Reason) does not explain why the embryo is small and situated in a groove (Assertion).

Question:
Statement I: Liverworts reproduce asexually by fragmentation or by the formation of specialised structures called gemmae, which are brown, multicellular, sexual buds
Statement II: The mosses have an elaborate mechanism of spore dispersal
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is incorrect: Liverworts reproduce asexually by fragmentation or by the formation of specialised structures called gemmae, which are green not brown, multicellular, asexual not sexual buds

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: In Arthropods, the body is covered by a proteinaceous exoskeleton and consists of head, thorax, and abdomen
Statement II: In insects, the mouth contains a file-like rasping organ for feeding, called the radula
Statement III: Annelids possess longitudinal and circular muscles which help in locomotion
Statement IV: The most distinctive feature of Echinoderms is the presence of a water canal system which helps in locomotion, capture and transport of food, and respiration
Options: Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements I and III are correct
Statements II, III and IV are correct
Only Statement III is correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: In Arthropods, the body is covered by a chitinous exoskeleton and consists of head, thorax, and abdomen
Statement II is incorrect: In Molluscs, the mouth contains a file-like rasping organ for feeding, called the radula
Statement IV is incorrect: The distinctive feature of Echinoderms is the Water Vascular System (ambulacral system). The "Water Canal System" is characteristic of Porifera

Question:
Assertion (A): In Arthropods, the circulatory system is of the closed type
Reason (R): Arthropods possess sensory organs like antennae, eyes (compound and simple), and statocysts for balance in aquatic forms
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is Incorrect: Arthropods have an open circulatory system i.e. blood vessels are absent/poorly developed and open into body cavities

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: For effective treatment of a disease, early diagnosis and understanding its pathophysiology is very important
Statement II: Using conventional methods of diagnosis (serum and urine analysis), early detection of pathogens is not possible
Statement III: PCR is now routinely used to detect HIV in suspected AIDS patients and to detect mutations in genes in suspected cancer patients
Statement IV: Recombinant DNA technology and ELISA are conventional diagnostic techniques used for late-stage detection of diseases
Options: Statements I, II, and III are correct
Statements I and III are correct
Statements II, III, and IV are correct
Statements I, III, and IV are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: Recombinant DNA technology, PCR, and ELISA are Molecular Diagnostic techniques, not "conventional" ones. They allow for early detection even when the concentration of the pathogen is very low.

Question: Identify A, B and C in the diagram:
Options: A: Proinsulin, B: Free C peptide, C: Insulin
A: Free C peptide, B: Proinsulin, C: Insulin
A: Insulin, B: Proinsulin, C: Free C peptide
A: Free C peptide, B: Insulin, C: Proinsulin
Solution: The correct matching is: A: Proinsulin, B: Free C peptide, C: Insulin

Question:
Assertion (A): The Human Genome Project was described as a "Mega Project"
Reason (R): It required the sequencing of approximately 3 x 109 bp with an estimated cost of 9 billion US dollars and necessitated the development of bioinformatics
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The scale of the project was massive, not just in terms of the number of base pairs, but also in terms of the workforce, cost, and the huge amount of data generated, which led to the requirement of high-speed computational devices for data storage and analysis.

Question:
Statement I: The cattle egret and grazing cattle in close association, is a classic example of symbiosis
Statement II: MacArther showed that five closely related species of warblers living on the same tree were able to avoid competition and co-exist due to behavioural differences in their foraging activities
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is incorrect: The cattle egret and grazing cattle in close association, is a classic example of commensalism. Statement II is correct and is the example of resource partitioning.

Question:
Match the Fungal Class in Column I with its common name in Column II:
Column IColumn II
APhycomycetes(i)Club fungi
BAscomycetes(ii)Imperfect fungi
CBasidiomycetes(iii)Sac fungi
DDeuteromycetes(iv)Algal fungi
Options: A-ii, B-iii, C-i, D-iv
A-iii, B-iv, C-ii, D-i
A-iv, B-i, C-iii, D-ii
A-iv, B-iii, C-i, D-ii
Solution: The correct matching is: A-iv, B-iii, C-i, D-ii

Question:
Assertion (A): Thorns of Bougainvillea and tendrils of Cucurbita are considered homologous organs
Reason (R): They are both modifications of the stem and indicate divergent evolution from a common ancestor
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Homology is based on common origin and divergent evolution. Since both structures arise from the axillary position as stem modifications, they share anatomical heritage despite their different functions (defense vs. climbing).

Question:
Read the following four statements and identify the correct sequence of True (T) or False (F):
Statement I: Abscisic acid (ABA) acts as a general plant growth inhibitor and an inhibitor of plant metabolism
Statement II: ABA stimulates the closure of stomata and increases the tolerance of plants to various kinds of stresses, earning it the name "stress hormone"
Statement III: ABA acts as an antagonist to GAs in many physiological situations
Statement IV: Plant growth regulators act in isolation and never interact with extrinsic factors like light or temperature to control plant growth
Select the correct option:
Options: I-T, II-F, III-F, IV-F
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-F
I-F, II-F, III-T, IV-T
I-T, II-F, III-T, IV-F
Solution:
Statement II is false: ABA stimulates the closure of stomata and increases the tolerance of plants to various kinds of stresses, earning it the name "stress hormone"
Statement IV is false: PGRs interact with both intrinsic (genetic) and extrinsic (environmental) factors like light and temperature (e.g., vernalisation, photoperiodism) to control growth.

Tags.Topic: Physiological Effects of Plant Growth Regulators

Tags.Subject: Botany
Tags.Topic: Physiological Effects of Plant Growth Regulators

Question:
Match Column I (Hormone) with Column II (Process Promoted):
Column IColumn II
A.Glucagon(1)Bone Resorption
B.Insulin(2)Glycogenolysis and Gluconeogenesis
C.PTH(3)Glycogenesis
D.Thymosin(4)Cell-mediated and Humoral Immunity
Solution: The correct matching is: A-2, B-3, C-1, D-4

Question:
Match the parts of the Female Reproductive System in Column I with their features in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Infundibulum(i)The widest part of the fallopian tube
B.Ampulla(ii)Funnel-shaped part closer to the ovary
C.Isthmus(iii)Finger-like projections that collect the ovum
D.Fimbriae(iv)Narrow part of the oviduct joining the uterus
Options: A-ii, B-i, C-iv, D-iii
A-i, B-ii, C-iii, D-iv
A-ii, B-iv, C-i, D-iii
A-iii, B-i, C-ii, D-iv
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-i, C-iv, D-iii

Question:
Select the correct answer after studying the following statements:
Statement I: The ovaries are the primary female sex organs that produces only the female gamete (ovum)
Statement II: Each ovary is covered by a thick epithelium which encloses the ovarian stroma, which is divided into a peripheral cortex and an inner medulla
Statement III: The oviduct (fallopian tube) is about 10–12 cm long and extends from the periphery of each ovary to the uterus.
Statement IV: The part of the fallopian tube closer to the ovary is the funnel-shaped infundibulum, which possesses finger-like projections called fimbriae.
Options: Statements I, III and IV are correct
Statements III and IV are correct
Statements I, II and III are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: The ovaries are the primary female sex organs that produces the female gamete (ovum) and several steroid hormones
Statement II is incorrect: Each ovary is covered by a thin epithelium (not thick) which encloses the ovarian stroma

Question:
Assertion (A): For a female parent producing unisexual flowers, the step of emasculation is not necessary in artificial hybridization
Reason (R): Emasculation is the removal of anthers from a bisexual flower bud before they dehisce and unisexual flowers for a female parent don’t possess stamens
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Because emasculation is specifically the removal of male parts (Reason), and a unisexual female flower lacks those parts, the procedure becomes redundant (Assertion).

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct set of statements:
Statement I: The stem develops from the plumule of the embryo of a germinating seed and bears nodes and internodes
Statement II: Leaves originate from shoot apical meristems and are arranged in a basipetal order
Statement III: A typical leaf consists of three main parts: leaf base, petiole, and lamina
Statement IV: In some leguminous plants, the leaf base may become swollen, which is called the pulvinus
Options: Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements I, III and IV are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: Leaves are arranged in an acropetal order (younger leaves at the top, older at the bottom), not basipetal.

Question:
Select the correct set of answer among the following:
Statement I: The number of chloroplasts varies from 1 per cell of the Chlamydomonas, a blue green alga to 20-40 per cell in the mesophyll
Statement II: The inner membrane of the mitochondria forms a number of infoldings called the cisternae
Statement III: Lysosomes, a part of the GERL complex, are membrane bound vesicular structures formed by the process of packaging in the golgi apparatus
Statement IV (ER): The ER often shows ribosomes attached to its inner luminal surface
Options: I-True, II-True, III-False, IV-False
I-False, II-False, III-True, IV-False
I-False, II-True, III-True, IV-True
I-True, II-False, III-True, IV-False
Solution:
Statement I, II and IV are False and cam be corrected as:
(I) The number of chloroplasts varies from 1 per cell of the Chlamydomonas, a green alga to 20-40 per cell in the mesophyll
(II) The inner membrane of the mitochondria forms a number of infoldings called the cristae
(IV) Ribosomes are attached to the outer surface of the ER, not the inner luminal surface.

Question: Match the concept in Column I with its molecular/phenotypic basis in Column II:
Options: A-i, B-ii, C-iii, D-iv
A-ii, B-iii, C-iv, D-i
A-ii, B-i, C-iv, D-iii
A-iv, B-iii, C-ii, D-i
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-i, C-iv, D-iii

Question:
Statement I: Binding of oxygen with haemoglobin is primarily related to the partial pressure of O2.
Statement II: High H+ concentration, high pH and high temperature in the tissues interfere with this binding and favour dissociation
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: High H+ concentration, low pH, and high temperature in the tissues interfere with this binding and favour dissociation

Question:
Select the correct statements:
Statement I: Each pectoral girdle consists of two bones: the clavicle and the scapula
Statement II: The dorsal, flat, triangular body of scapula has a slightly elevated ridge called the acromion
Statement III: The clavicle is commonly called the collar bone
Statement IV: The glenoid cavity, which articulates with the head of the humerus to form the shoulder joint, is a depression located below the acromion
Options: I and II only
II and III only
I, III and IV only
All of the above
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: The dorsal, flat, triangular body of scapula has a slightly elevated ridge called the spine not the acromion

Question:
Frog's heart when taken out of the body continues to beat for some time. Select the option containing the correct statements:
I. Frog is not a poikilotherm
II. Frog does not have any coronary circulation
III. Heart is ‘myogenic’ in nature
IV. Heart is autoexcitable
Options: Only III
Only IV
I and II
III and IV
Solution:
Statements I and II are incorrect and can be corrected as:
- Frogs are poikilotherms or cold-blooded
- Frogs possess coronary circulation

Question:
Assertion (A): The midbrain of a frog is characterized by a pair of optic lobes
Reason (R): The brain is enclosed in a bony structure called the cranium
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are anatomically correct facts. However, the protection of the brain by the cranium (Reason) has no causal link to the presence of optic lobes in the midbrain (Assertion). They are independent facts about the nervous system.

Question:
Statement I: RBCs in most mammals are nucleated and biconvex in shape
Statement II: RBCs have an average life span of 120 days after which they are destroyed in the liver (graveyard of RBCs).
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: RBCs are devoid of nucleus in most of the mammals and are biconcave in shape.
Statement II: RBCs have an average life span of 120 days after which they are destroyed in the liver (graveyard of RBCs).

Question:
Match Column I with Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Family(1)Assemblage of families which exhibit a few similar characters
B.Order(2)Group of related genera with less number of similarities as compared to genus and species
C.Class(3)Group of related orders
D.Phylum(4)Includes classes like Amphibia, Reptilia, and Aves
Solution: The correct matching is: A-2, B-1, C-3, D-4

Question:
Statement I: Ladybird and Dragonflies are used to get rid of aphids and mosquitoes, respectively
Statement II: Trichoderma species are symbiotic bacteria that are effective biocontrol agents of several plant pathogens
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: Trichoderma are free-living fungi, not symbiotic bacteria. They are common in the root ecosystems.

Tags.Subject: Botany
Tags.Chapter: Strategies for enhancement in food production

Question:
Read the statements given below and choose the correct option
Statement I: Sequoia is one of the tallest tree species and belongs to Gymnosperms
Statement II: Wolffia is microscopic and is the smallest Angiosperm
Statement III: In Gymnosperms, the nucellus is protected by envelopes and the composite structure is called an ovary
Statement IV: In Angiosperms, the seeds are enclosed by fruits
Options: Statements I, II and IV are correct
Statements I and III are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement III is incorrect: In Gymnosperms, the composite structure of the nucellus protected by envelopes is called an ovule, not an ovary. (Gymnosperms lack ovaries)

Question:
Read the following statements and choose the correct sequence of True (T) and False (F):
Statement I: Transgenic mice are being developed for use in testing the safety of vaccines before they are used on humans (e.g., polio vaccine)
Statement II: For chemical safety testing (toxicity testing), transgenic animals are made that carry genes which make them more resistant to toxic substances
Statement III: The Indian Parliament has recently cleared the second amendment of the Indian Patents Bill, which takes biopiracy and patent terms into consideration
Statement IV: Over 95 percent of all existing transgenic animals are mice
Options: I-T, II-F, III-T, IV-T
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-F
I-F, II-F, III-T, IV-T
I-F, II-T, III-F, IV-T
Solution: Statement II is False: In toxicity testing, transgenic animals are made more sensitive (not more resistant) to toxic substances so that results can be obtained in less time.

Question: Match the processing step in Column I with its specific action in Column II:
Options: A-i, B-ii, C-iv, D-iii
A-ii, B-iv, C-i, D-iii
A-iv, B-iii, C-ii, D-i
A-ii, B-i, C-iii, D-iv
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-i, C-iii, D-iv

Question:
Read the following statements carefully and select the correct answer among the following:
Statement I: A classical example of point mutation is a change of single base pair in the gene for β-globin chain that results in the change of amino acid residue valine to glutamate
Statement II: Insertion or deletion of one or two bases changes the reading frame from the point of insertion or deletion, referred to as frame-shift insertion or deletion mutations
Statement III: Insertion or deletion of three or its multiple bases inserts or deletes one or multiple codons, hence one or multiple amino acids, and the reading frame remains unaltered from that point onwards
Statement IV: In the lac operon, the y gene codes for transacetylase, which increases permeability of the cell to beta-galactosides
Options: Statements I and IV are incorrect
Statements II and III are not incorrect
Statements I, II, and III are correct
Both (a) and (b)
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: A classical example of point mutation is a change of single base pair in the gene for β-globin chain that results in the change of amino acid residue glutamate to valine
Statement IV is incorrect: The y gene codes for permease. It is the a gene that codes for transacetylase.

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: Adaptive radiation is the process of evolution of different species in a given geographical area starting from a point and literally radiating to other areas
Statement II: Darwin’s Finches represent one of the best examples of adaptive radiation where many varieties evolved from the original seed-eating feature
Statement III: Australian marsupials are an example of adaptive radiation where a number of marsupials, each different from the other, evolved from an ancestral stock within the Australian island continent
Statement IV: Placental mammals in Australia do not show adaptive radiation or any similarity to corresponding marsupials
Options: Statements I, II, and III are correct
Statements I and IV are correct
Statements II, III, and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: Placental mammals in Australia also exhibit adaptive radiation. Furthermore, when more than one adaptive radiation occurs in an isolated geographical area (representing different habitats), it results in convergent evolution, where placental mammals show similarities to corresponding marsupials (e.g., Placental Wolf and Tasmanian Wolf).

Question:
Look at the diagram carefully:

Which organisms are primarily responsible for the initial decomposition of fallen leaves in the diagram?
Options: Earthworms and bacteria
Fungi and bacteria
Insects and animals
Soil mites and fungi
Solution: The correct matching is: Fungi and bacteria

Question: Given below is the representation of the extent of global diversity of invertebrates. What groups the four portions (A-D) represent, respectively?
Options: A: Insects, B: Crustaceans, C: Other animal groups, D:Molluscs
A: Crustaceans, B: Insects, C: Molluscs, D: Other animal groups
A: Molluscs, B: Other animal groups, C: Crustaceans, D: Insects
A: Insects, B: Molluscs, C: Crustaceans, D: Other animal groups
Solution: The correct matching is: A: Insects, B: Molluscs, C: Crustaceans, D: Other animal groups

Question:
Assertion (A): Parasympathetic neural signals decrease the cardiac output
Reason (R): Parasympathetic neural signals decrease the rate of heartbeat and the speed of conduction of action potential
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Reason is True and Correct Explanation: The mechanism by which the output is reduced is precisely by slowing down the heart rate and the conduction speed of the action potential.

Question:
Assertion (A): Cytokinin can be referred to as the "anti-stress hormone"
Reason (R): Cytokinin stimulates the opening of stomata working antagonistic to ABA which closes the stomata
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are true and the reason justifies the assertion statement
