B. Thomas Hunt Morgan

Parallelism between gene and chromosome behaviour

C. Alfred Sturtevant

Discovery of the X-body (later known as X- chromosome)

D. Henking

First genetic maps based on recombination frequency
Options: A-iv, B-iii, C-ii, D-i
A-i, B-ii, C-iv, D-iii
A-ii, B-iii, C-i, D-iv
A-ii, B-i, C-iv, D-iii
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-i, C-iv, D-iii

Question:
Assertion (A): Vessels are the main water-conducting elements in Gymnosperms
Reason (R): The radial conduction of water takes place by the ray parenchymatous cells
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is Incorrect: Gymnosperms lack vessels in their xylem. Their main water-conducting elements are tracheids. (Presence of vessels is a characteristic feature of Angiosperms).

Question:
Statement I: Collenchyma cells are living cells that are much thickened at the corners due to the deposition of cellulose, hemicellulose, and pectin
Statement II: Sclerenchyma fibers are living cells with thin cell walls and large intercellular spaces
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: Sclerenchyma fibers are dead cells (at maturity), have highly thickened (lignified) walls, and generally lack intercellular spaces.

Question:
Match Column I (Gland/Structure) with Column II (Location/Feature):
Column IColumn II
A.Pituitary Gland(1)Located on the dorsal side of the forebrain
B.Pineal Gland(2)Located in a bony cavity called Sella turcica
C.Hypothalamus(3)Ductless glands regulating body functions
D.Endocrine Glands(4)Basal part of diencephalon, forebrain
Solution: The correct matching is: A-2, B-1, C-4, D-3

Question: Match the Column I with the correct description in Column II:
Options: A-iii, B-ii, C-iv, D-i
A-i, B-iv, C-iii, D-ii
A-iv, B-i, C-ii, D-iii
A-ii, B-iii, C-i, D-iv
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-iii, C-i, D-iv

Question: Match the disorder in Column I with the specific molecular defect in Column II:
Options: A-i, B-ii, C-iii, D-iv
A-iii, B-iv, C-ii, D-i
A-ii, B-i, C-iv, D-iii
A-iv, B-iii, C-ii, D-i
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-i, C-iv, D-iii

Question:
Study the statements (1-3) given below and select the correct one w.r.t. restriction enzymes:
Statement 1: Today we know more than 900 restriction enzymes that have been isolated from over 230 strains of bacteria
Statement 2: All restriction enzymes recognize same recognition sequences
Statement 3: They add methyl groups to DNA for restricting the growth of bacteriophage
Options: Only 1 and 3
Only 1
Only 1 and 2
Only 3
Solution: Statement 2 is incorrect: All restriction enzymes recognize different recognition sequences.

Question:
Assertion (A): If a plant is infected with a virus, it is still possible to obtain a healthy, virus-free plant through tissue culture
Reason (R): The apical and axillary meristems of an infected plant are always free of viruses
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The specific virus-free nature of the meristem (Reason) is the biological fact that enables the recovery of healthy plants (Assertion).

Tags.Subject: Botany
Tags.Chapter: Strategies for enhancement in food production

Question:
Assertion (A): Peptide hormones like insulin do not enter the target cell but generate second messengers
Reason (R): Peptide hormones are water-soluble and interact with membrane-bound receptors present on the cell surface
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The Reason correctly explains the Assertion. Because peptide hormones are water-soluble (lipid-insoluble), they cannot cross the lipid bilayer of the cell membrane. Therefore, they must bind to surface receptors and rely on a second messenger system to transmit the signal intracellularly.

Question:
Read the following statements carefully and select the correct answer among the following:
Statement I: No population of any species in nature has at its disposal unlimited resources to permit exponential growth
Statement II: Population size, technically called population density (designated as N), is measured in numbers only
Statement III: The logistic growth model is also known as the Verhulst-Pearl Logistic Growth and is represented by a J-shaped curve
Statement IV: A population growing in a habitat with limited resources shows initially a lag phase, followed by phases of acceleration and deceleration
Options: Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Statements I and IV are correct
Statements II, III, and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement II is incorrect: Population size, technically called population density (designated as N), need not necessarily be measured in numbers only
Statement III is incorrect: Logistic growth model is represented by a Sigmoid (S-shaped) curve, not a J-shaped curve. The J-shaped curve is characteristic of exponential growth.

Question:
Assertion (A): Abrupt discontinuation of regular drug intake leads to Withdrawal Syndrome
Reason (R): Withdrawal Syndrome is characterized by anxiety, shakiness, nausea, and sweating, which are relieved when use is resumed
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are true and represent the definition and symptoms of dependence as stated in the NCERT. However, the Reason (listing the symptoms) describes what the syndrome looks like, but it does not technically explain the biological why (the body's homeostatic dependency) behind the Assertion. Therefore, (R) is a correct statement but not the explanation for (A).

Question:
Statement I: The immediate adverse effects of drugs and alcohol abuse are manifested as reckless behaviour, vandalism, and violence
Statement II: Excessive doses of drugs may lead to coma and death due to respiratory failure, heart failure, or cerebral haemorrhage
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: Evidences in the form of fossils are called embryological evidences
Statement II: Evolution by natural selection is a process that has occurred over millions of years
Statement III: The universe is very old – almost 13.8 billion years old
Statement IV: The UV rays from the sun broke up water into Hydrogen and Oxygen and the heavier H2 settled
Options: Statements I and III are correct
Statements II and III are correct
Statements I, II, and III are correct
Statements II, III, and IV are correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: Evidences in the form of fossils are called paleontological evidences.
Statement IV is incorrect: The UV rays from the sun broke up water into Hydrogen and Oxygen and the lighter H2 escaped

Tags.Subject: Zoology
Tags.Topic: Evolution of Life Forms - A Theory

Question:
Assertion (A): 'Insects' represent a taxon or a category
Reason (R): All insects share common features like jointed appendages
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Insects (Class Insecta) is a recognizable group because its members share distinct features (jointed appendages). This shared feature is the basis (explanation) for recognizing them as a distinct concrete biological object (taxon).

Question:
Assertion (A): Giraffes developed long necks by stretching them to reach leaves on tall trees, and this acquired character was passed on to succeeding generations
Reason (R): Lamarck's theory of evolution is based on the concept of "use and disuse" of organs
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both are true. Lamarck argued that an organ used frequently would grow and strengthen, while an unused one would deteriorate. He believed these "acquired traits" were inheritable, though this part of his theory was later disproven by modern genetics.

Question:
Statement I: Fish have a 2-chambered heart with an atrium and a ventricle, and the heart pumps out deoxygenated blood
Statement II: All amphibians and reptiles without any exception have a 3-chambered heart with two atria and a single ventricle, exhibiting incomplete double circulation
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: Amphibians and reptiles (except crocodiles) have a 3-chambered heart with two atria and a single ventricle, exhibiting incomplete double circulation

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: Spermatogenesis starts at the age of puberty due to a significant increase in the secretion of gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH)
Statement II: Luteinizing hormone (LH) acts on the sertoli cells and stimulates the secretion of some factors which help in the process of spermiogenesis
Statement III: Each secondary spermatocyte undergoes the second meiotic division to produce four haploid spermatids
Statement IV: The process of transformation of spermatids into spermatozoa is called spermiogenesis
Options: Statements I, II and IV are correct
Statements I, III and IV are correct
Statements II and III are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect. According to NCERT, LH acts on the Leydig cells to stimulate the synthesis and secretion of androgens. It is Follicle Stimulating Hormone (FSH) that acts on the Sertoli cells to stimulate factors for spermiogenesis

Question:
Statement I: The quaternary structure is formed when a single long protein chain folds upon itself like a hollow woollen ball.
Statement II: Tertiary structure is absolutely necessary for many biological activities of proteins
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect.
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct.
Solution: Statement I is Incorrect: The folding of a long protein chain upon itself like a hollow woollen ball gives rise to the Tertiary structure, not Quaternary. Quaternary structure refers to the assembly of multiple subunits.

Question:
Statement I: The scutellum is the shield-shaped single cotyledon found in the embryos of the grass family
Statement II: In monocot embryos, the root cap is enclosed in an undifferentiated sheath called the coleoptile
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: The sheath enclosing the radical and root cap is the coleorhiza. The coleoptile encloses the shoot apex and leaf primordia.

B. Integral Protein2. Lies on the surface of the membrane and is easily separable
C. Polar Head3. Allows for the lateral movement of proteins within the bilayer
D. Quasi-Fluid Lipid4. Faces the outer aqueous environment
The diagram below shows the diploblastic and triploblastic germ layers in the animals. Identify the correct option in which they are found:

A–Annelida, B–Porifera
A–Coelenterates, B–Platyhelminthes
A–Porifera, B–Cnidaria

Column IColumn II

B.Acrosome(ii)Site of fertilisation in the fallopian tube
C.Ampulla(iii)Unequal division completing female meiosis

Question:
Statement I: In a floral formula, G with a line drawn above indicates an superior ovary
Statement II: Adhesion is indicated by an arc drawn above the symbols of the floral parts
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer among the following:
Statement I: In Chlorophyceae, the food is stored in the form of starch, and the cell wall is made of an inner layer of cellulose and an outer layer of pectose
Statement II: In Phaeophyceae, the vegetative cells have a cellulosic wall usually covered on the outside by a gelatinous coating of algin
Statement III: In Rhodophyceae, the cell wall is made of cellulose, pectin and polysulphate esters
Statement IV: In Bryophytes, the cell wall of the gametophyte is made of chitin
Options: Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements I and II are correct
Statements II, III and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: Bryophytes are plants, and their cell walls are made of cellulose, not chitin (chitin is found in Fungi).

Solution: A-2, B-1, C-4, D-3

Question:
Statement I: In Haemophilia, which is a sex-linked recessive disease, a single protein that is a part of the cascade of proteins involved in the clotting of blood is affected
Statement II: The family pedigree of Queen Victoria shows a number of haemophilic descendants as she was affected by the disease
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: The family pedigree of Queen Victoria shows a number of haemophilic descendants as she was a carrier of the disease

Question:
Read the following statements carefully and select the correct answer among the following
Statement I: In vector pBR322 the ampicillin resistance gene has recognition sites for Pst I and Pvu I.
Statement II: The tetracycline resistance gene in pBR322 has restriction sites for BamH I and Sal I
Statement III: Presence of more than one recognition site for a single restriction enzyme within a vector is preferred to simplify gene cloning
Statement IV: A foreign DNA is ligated at a restriction site present in one of the two antibiotic resistance genes to facilitate selection
Options: Statements I, II and IV are correct
Statements I and III are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement III is incorrect: The presence of more than one recognition site within the vector will generate several fragments, which will complicate gene cloning.

Tags.Subject: Zoology
Tags.Topic: Tools of Recombinant DNA Technology - Cloning Vectors

Question:
Assertion (A): In Griffith's experiments, mice injected with a mixture of heat-killed S-strain and live R-strain bacteria died of pneumonia
Reason (R): The R-strain bacteria had transitioned into S-strain by absorbing some "transforming principle" from the heat-killed S-strain
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Griffith observed that heat-killed S-strain alone didn't kill the mice, and neither did live R-strain. However, when combined, the live R-strain "transformed" into virulent S-strain by picking up genetic material (the transforming principle) from the dead S-strain, causing the mice to die.

Question:
Consider the following four statements regarding the specific functions of pituitary hormones:
Statement 1: TSH stimulates the synthesis and secretion of testosterone from the testes
Statement 2: ACTH stimulates the synthesis and secretion of steroid hormones called glucocorticoids from the adrenal medulla
Statement 3: LH and FSH stimulate gonadal activity and hence are called gonadotrophins
Statement 4: Vasopressin acts mainly at the kidney and stimulates resorption of water and electrolytes, thereby reducing loss of water through urine
Which of the following options is correct?
Options: Statements 1 and 2 are incorrect
Only Statement 3 is correct
Statements 1, 2 and 4 are correct
All statements 1, 2, 3 and 4 are correct
Solution:
Statement 1 is incorrect: TSH stimulates the synthesis and secretion of thyroid hormones from the thyroid gland not testosterone from the testes
Statement 2 is incorrect: ACTH stimulates the synthesis and secretion of steroid hormones called glucocorticoids from the adrenal cortex not medulla

Question: Identify the given figure and select the correct option:
Options: It is marine water plankton
It is a saprophytic protist
It is parasitic predator believed to but primary relative of animals
Ciliated protozoan
Solution: The correct labelling is: It is a saprophytic protist as it represents a slime mould

Question:
Match the items in Column I with their specific feature in Column II.
Column IColumn II
A.Oils(1)Found in neural tissues
B.Fats(2)Structures like cell membranes
C.Complex Lipids(3)Lower melting point (e.g., Gingelly oil)
D.Phospholipids(4)Higher melting point
Solution:
Oils have a lower melting point and remain fluid in winters.
Fats have a higher melting point and are solid in winters.
Complex Lipids: Some tissues especially the neural tissues have lipids with more complex structures
Phospholipids: Are major structural components of cell membranes.

Question: Match the terms in Column I with their descriptions in Column II:

Question:
Statement I: When a flower can be divided into two equal radial halves in any radial plane passing through the centre, it is said to be zygomorphic
Statement II: Cassia, bean, and gulmohur are examples of asymmetrical flowers
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: Radial symmetry is called actinomorphic (not zygomorphic).

Statement II is incorrect: Cassia, bean, and gulmohur are examples of zygomorphic (bilateral symmetry) flowers.

Question:
Statement I: The hypodermis in a dicotyledonous stem consists of sclerenchymatous cells
Statement II: The vascular bundles in a monocotyledonous stem are 'closed', meaning they lack cambium
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is Incorrect: The hypodermis in a Dicot stem consists of Collenchyma cells. (Sclerenchymatous hypodermis is found in Monocot stems).

Question:
Read the statements given below and choose the correct option:
Statement I: The vascular bundles in a Dicot stem are arranged in a ring; this 'ring' arrangement is a characteristic of dicot stems
Statement II: In a Monocot stem, the vascular bundles are scattered, conjoint, and closed
Statement III: The hypodermis in a Dicot stem consists of sclerenchymatous cells, while in a Monocot stem, it consists of collenchymatous cells
Statement IV: Water-containing cavities are present within the vascular bundles of a Monocot stem
Options: Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements I, II and IV are correct
Statements II and III are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement III is incorrect: The hypodermis in a Dicot stem is collenchymatous, whereas the hypodermis in a Monocot stem is sclerenchymatous.

Question:
Statement I: The air bladder in bony fishes provides buoyancy and prevents them from sinking
Statement II: The heart is two-chambered in all fishes, consisting of one atrium and one ventricle
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct

Question:
Statement I: Totipotency allows regenerating whole plants from seeds only
Statement II: Meristem culture allows recovery of virus-free plants from infected plants
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is Incorrect: This capacity to generate a whole plant from any cell/explant is called totipotency

Question:

Question:
Consider the following statements regarding respiratory volumes:
Statement I: Tidal Volume (TV) is the volume of air inspired or expired during normal respiration, approximately 500 ml
Statement II: Inspiratory Reserve Volume (IRV) averages between 1000 ml to 1100 ml
Statement III: Expiratory Reserve Volume (ERV) is the additional volume of air a person can expire by a forcible expiration
Statement IV: Residual Volume (RV) can be easily measured using a standard spirometer
Which of the above statements are CORRECT?
Options: I, II and III
I and III only
II and IV only
III and IV only
Solution:
Statement II is Incorrect: IRV averages between 2500 ml to 3000 ml
Statement IV is Incorrect: Residual Volume (RV) cannot be measured by a spirometer because it is the air remaining in lungs even after forcible expiration.

Question:
Match the Disorder in Column I with its Description in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Asthma(1)Proliferation of fibrous tissues
B.Emphysema(2)Inflammation of bronchi and bronchioles
C.Silicosis(3)Alveolar walls are damaged
D.Cigarette Smoking(4)Major cause of Emphysema
Solution: The correct matching is: A-2, B-3, C-1, D-4

Question:
Assertion (A): The content of the nucleolus is continuous with the rest of the nucleoplasm.
Reason (R): The nucleolus is not a membrane-bound structure.
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
Solution:
Assertion is True: There is no barrier between the nucleolus and the nucleoplasm.
Reason is True: It lacks a limiting membrane.
Explanation: The absence of a boundary membrane (Reason) is the direct cause of the physical continuity (Assertion).

Question: Which of the following options is correct with respect to the given figure showing different zones of a typical root?
Options: Part B mainly helps in absorption of water
Quiescent centre is present in part B
Part A is most suitable for anatomical studies of root
Differentiation of cells can be observed in part C
Solution: The correct matching is: Part A is the zone of maturation is most suitable for anatomical studies of root

Question:
Assertion (A): All copulations lead to fertilisation and pregnancy
Reason (R): Fertilisation can only occur if the ovum and sperms are transported simultaneously to the ampullary region
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is False: Most copulations do not result in pregnancy because the window of opportunity for the egg and sperm to meet is very narrow

Question:
State whether the following statements are True (T) or False (F):
A. The Malpighian corpuscle, PCT, and DCT of the nephron are situated in the cortical region of the kidney
B. Juxtamedullary nephrons have a short loop of Henle that extends only very little into the medulla
C. The Vasa recta is a minute vessel of the peritubular capillary network that runs parallel to the Henle's loop
D. Vasa recta is absent or highly reduced in cortical nephrons
Options: I-T, II-F, III-T, IV-T
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-F
I-F, II-F, III-T, IV-T
I-F, II-T, III-F, IV-T
Solution: False: Cortical nephrons have a short loop. Juxtamedullary nephrons have a very long loop of Henle that runs deep into the medulla.

Question:
Statement I: Presence of glucose (Glycosuria) and ketone bodies (Ketonuria) in urine are indicative of Diabetes insipidus
Statement II: On average, 25-30 mg of urea is excreted out per day by an adult human.
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution:
Statement I is Incorrect: These conditions are indicative of Diabetes mellitus, not Diabetes insipidus
Statement II is Incorrect: An adult human excretes 25-30 gm (grams) of urea per day, not mg (milligrams)

Question:
Read the following statements and choose the correct option:
Statement I: Often flowers of animal pollinated plants are specifically adapted for a number of animals to ensure pollination
Statement II: Flowers pollinated by flies and beetles secrete foul odours to attract these insects
Statement III: Nectar and pollen grains are the usual floral rewards provided to insect pollinators
Statement IV: In some species, floral rewards are in providing safe places to lay eggs; an example is the tallest flower of Amorphophallus
Options: Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Statements II, III and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement I is incorrect: Often flowers of animal pollinated plants are specifically adapted for a particular species of animal

Question:
Match the sub-stage in Column I with its specific characteristic event in Column II.
Column IColumn II
A.Zygotene(i)Terminalisation of chiasmata
B.Pachytene(ii)Appearance of recombination nodules
C.Diplotene(iii)Formation of synaptonemal complex
D.Diakinesis(iv) Dissolution of synaptonemal complex and appearance of X-shaped structures

Question:
Statement I: The corpus luteum secretes large amounts of progesterone which is essential for the maintenance of the endometrium
Statement II: In the absence of fertilisation, the corpus luteum grows further in size to prepare for pregnancy
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: In the absence of fertilisation, the corpus luteum degenerates. This causes the disintegration of the endometrium, leading to menstruation.

Question:
Assertion (A): In human females, oogenesis is initiated during the embryonic development stage
Reason (R): No more oogonia are formed and added after birth
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The fact that the total supply of oogonia is fixed before birth (Reason) is the biological basis for why the process must be initiated during embryonic life (Assertion).

Question:
Read the following statements regarding sex determination in honeybees and select the correct answer:
Statement I: Sex determination in honeybees is based on the number of sets of chromosomes an individual receives
Statement II: An offspring formed from the union of a sperm and an egg develops as a female (queen or worker)
Statement III: Unfertilized eggs develop into males (drones) by means of parthenogenesis
Statement IV: Males have 32 chromosomes while females have 16 chromosomes
Options: Statements I and II are correct
Statements I, II, and III are correct
Statements III and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: The ploidy is reversed: females are diploid (32 chromosomes) and males are haploid (16 chromosomes).

Question:
Statement I: In frogs, the kidneys are compact, dark red and bean like structures situated a little anteriorly in the body cavity on both sides of vertebral column
Statement II: Frog has different types of sense organs, namely organs of touch (sensory papillae), taste (taste buds), smell (nasal epithelium), vision (eyes) and hearing (tympanum with internal ears); out of which eyes and internal ears are cellular aggregations around nerve endings
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution:
Statement I: In frogs, the kidneys are compact, dark red and bean like structures situated a little posteriorly in the body cavity on both sides of vertebral column
Statement II: Frog has different types of sense organs, namely organs of touch (sensory papillae), taste (taste buds), smell (nasal epithelium), vision (eyes) and hearing (tympanum with internal ears); out of which eyes and internal ears are well-organised structures and the rest are cellular aggregations around nerve endings

Question: In the given diagram of mechanism of breathing, what does A, B and C depict?
Options: A–Air goes inside to lungs, B–Ribs and sternum returned to original position, C– Diaphragm contracted
A–Air expelled from lungs, B–Ribs and sternum returned to original position, C–Diaphragm relaxed and arched upward
A–Air expelled from lungs, B–Ribs and sternum go upward, C–Diaphragm relaxed and arched upward
A–Air goes inside to lungs, B–Ribs and sternum go upward, C–Diaphragm relaxed and arched upward
Solution: Relaxation of the diaphragm and the inter-costal muscles returns the diaphragm and sternum to their normal positions and reduce the thoracic volume and thereby the pulmonary volume. This leads to an increase in intra-pulmonary pressure to slightly above the atmospheric pressure causing the expulsion of air from the lungs, i.e., expiration

Question:
Statement I: In mitosis, since the number of chromosomes in the parent and progeny cells is the same, it is also called as equational division
Statement II: M phase is the least dramatic period of the cell cycle
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: M phase is the most dramatic period of the cell cycle, involving a major reorganisation of virtually all components of the cell.

Question:
Select the correct answer after studying the following statements:
Statement I: As the anther develops, the cells of the sporogenous tissue undergo meiosis to form microspore dyads
Statement II: The hard outer layer of the pollen grain is called exine, which is made up of sporopollenin, one of the most resistant organic materials known
Statement III: The exine has prominent apertures called germ pores where intine is absent
Statement IV: The inner wall of the pollen grain is the intine, which is a thick and discontinuous layer made of sporopollenin
Options: Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements I, III and IV are correct
Statements I, II and III are incorrect
Statements I, III and IV are incorrect
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: As the anther develops, the cells of the sporogenous tissue undergo meiosis to form microspore tetrads
Statement III is incorrect: The exine has prominent apertures called germ pores where sporopollenin is absent
Statement IV is incorrect: The intine is a thin and continuous layer made of cellulose and pectin, not sporopollenin

Question:
Statement I: The coding strand in a transcription unit does not code for anything but is used as a reference point while defining the regions of the unit
Statement II: The promoter is a DNA sequence that provides a binding site for RNA polymerase and is located towards the 5' end (upstream) of the coding strand
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct

Question:
Assertion (A): Tropical rain forests like the Amazon are often called the 'Lungs of the planet'
Reason (R): Broadly utilitarian arguments for conserving biodiversity emphasize ecosystem services like the production of oxygen through photosynthesis
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are true. The Amazon rain forest produces a massive amount of the earth's oxygen, and this is indeed a "broadly utilitarian" service. However, the definition of broadly utilitarianism is not the reason why the forest is called the 'Lungs of the planet'; it is simply the category that the service falls into

Question:
Assertion (A): In a monohybrid cross, the F1 generation shows only one of the two contrasting parental traits
Reason (R): According to the Law of Dominance, in a dissimilar pair of factors, one member of the pair dominates the other
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: When Mendel crossed tall (TT) and dwarf (tt) plants, all F1 plants were tall (Tt). This is because the "Tall" factor is dominant over the "Dwarf" factor. The Law of Dominance specifically explains why only the dominant trait is expressed in the heterozygous condition of the F1 generation.

Question:

Question:
Read the following statements:
Statement I: Coronary Artery Disease (CAD), often referred to as atherosclerosis, affects the vessels that supply blood to the heart skeletal muscle
Statement II: In CAD, the lumen of the arteries becomes narrower due to deposits of calcium, fat, cholesterol, and fibrous tissues.
Statement III: Angina pectoris consists of acute chest pain appearing when not enough oxygen is reaching the heart muscle
Statement IV: Angina occurs exclusively in elderly men and never affects women or younger individuals
Which combination represents the CORRECT statements?
Options: I and II only
I, II and III only
III and IV only
II and III only
Solution:
Statement I is Incorrect: Coronary Artery Disease (CAD), often referred to as atherosclerosis, affects the vessels that supply blood to the heart cardiac muscle
Statement IV is Incorrect: Angina can occur in men and women of any age, although it is more common among the middle-aged and elderly.

Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Reason is False: Leydig cells are located in the intertubular spaces (outside the seminiferous tubules), not inside them. The cells inside the tubules are male germ cells and Sertoli cells.

Question:
Statement I: The dura mater is the innermost layer of the cranial meninges and is in direct contact with the brain tissue
Statement II: The pia mater is the tough, fibrous outer layer of the cranial meninges
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution:
Statement I is Incorrect: The dura mater is the tough, fibrous outer layer of the cranial meninges
Statement II is Incorrect: The pia mater is the innermost layer (thin and vascular), not the outer layer. The descriptions have been swapped.

Question:
Assertion (A): In tea plantations and hedge-making, decapitation is a widely practiced method
Reason (R): Removal of the shoot tip removes the source of Auxin, which allows growth of lateral buds and make the plant bushy
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The Reason correctly explains the Assertion. Apical dominance is caused by auxin produced in the shoot tip. Removing the tip stops this inhibition, promoting lateral branching, which is the specific goal in tea plucking and hedge trimming.

Question:
Statement I: The thymus gland is located on the dorsal side of the aorta
Statement II: The size of the thymus gland increases as age advances, enhancing immunity in the elderly
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution:
Statement I is Incorrect: The thymus is located on the ventral side of the aorta (behind the sternum).
Statement II is Incorrect: The thymus degenerates in old individuals, leading to decreased thymosin production and weaker immune responses.

Question:

Statement III: Methanogens are responsible for the production of methane (biogas) from the dung of ruminant animals
Statement IV: The vast majority of bacteria are autotrophs, meaning they do not depend on other organisms for food
Options: I-T, II-T, III-T, IV-F
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-F
I-F, II-F, III-T, IV-T
I-F, II-T, III-F, IV-T
Solution: Statement IV is False: The vast majority of bacteria are heterotrophs, i.e., they depend on other organisms or dead organic matter for food; they do not synthesise their own food.

Question:
Read the following statements and choose the correct sequence of True (T) and False (F):
Statement I: Reproductive health in a society refers only to healthy reproductive organs with normal functions
Statement II: "Saheli"—a new oral contraceptive for the females—was developed by scientists at Central Drug Research Institute (CDRI), Karnal
Statement III: Amniocentesis is misused as a foetal sex determination test based on the chromosomal pattern in the amniotic fluid surrounding the developing embryo

Question:

Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct

Question:
Assertion (A): A sub-metacentric chromosome has one shorter arm and one longer arm and is L shaped.
Reason (R): In sub-metacentric chromosomes, the centromere is situated slightly away from the middle of the chromosome
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution:
Assertion is True: This is the defining characteristic of the arms in a sub-metacentric chromosome which makes it resemble the alphabet L
Reason is True: This describes the position of the centromere
Explanation: The position of the centromere (slightly off centre) is the direct cause of the unequal arm lengths described in the Assertion. Therefore, R is the correct explanation of A.

Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect

Question:
Statement I: Human insulin is synthesized as a pro-hormone which contains an extra stretch called the C-peptide
Statement II: The C-peptide is removed during maturation into insulin and is present in the mature insulin available in the blood
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: The C-peptide is removed during maturation; therefore, it is not present in the mature insulin molecule. This was the main challenge in producing functional insulin via rDNA technology.

Question:

Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The presence of memory cells (Reason) is the biological basis for why the subsequent response is so intensified (Assertion).

Question:
Statement I: During ice age between 75,00-10,00 years ago modern Homo sapiens arose
Statement II: The Neanderthal man with a brain size of 14000cc lived in near east and central Asia between 1,00,000-40,000 years back.
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: During ice age between 75,000-10,000 years ago modern Homo sapiens arose.
Statement II is incorrect: The Neanderthal man with a brain size of 1400cc lived in near east and central Asia between 1,00,000-40,000 years back. They used hides to protect their body and buried their dead. Homo sapiens arose in Africa and moved across continents and developed into distinct races.

Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: According to NCERT, micro-injection is the method specifically used for animal cells (injecting directly into the nucleus).

Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: In Vallisneria, the female flower reaches the surface of water by a long stalk, and the male flowers or pollen grains are released on the surface of water.

A is false but R is true

Question:

Three
Four

Question:
Statement I: Morgan and his group knew that the genes were located on the X chromosome
Statement II: Genetic maps are extensively used as a starting point in the sequencing of whole genomes, such as in the Human Genome Project
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both statements are correct. Sturtevant, Morgan's student, pioneered the use of recombination frequencies to "map" genes linearly on a chromosome. This remains a cornerstone of modern genomic research.

Question:

Question:

Question:

Statement II: In all Pteridophytes, the sporophylls form distinct compact structures called strobili or cones

Statements II and IV are correct

Solution: Statement II is incorrect: Distinct compact structures called strobili or cones are formed in some cases (e.g., Selaginella, Equisetum), but not in all Pteridophytes.

Question:
Consider the following statements regarding the steps of Glycolysis:
Statement I: Sucrose is converted into glucose and fructose by the enzyme invertase, and these two disaccharides readily enter the glycolytic pathway
Statement II: Glucose-6-phosphate is isomerised to Fructose-6-phosphate
Statement III: The conversion of Fructose-6-phosphate to Fructose-1, 6-bisphosphate is a reversible reaction that produces one ATP
Statement IV: The splitting of Fructose-1, 6-bisphosphate results in two triose phosphates: Dihydroxy acetone phosphate (DHAP) and 3-Phosphoglyceraldehyde (PGAL)
Which of the above statements are CORRECT?
Options: I, II and IV only
II and IV only
I and III only
All of the above
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: Sucrose is converted into glucose and fructose by the enzyme invertase, and these two monosaccharides readily enter the glycolytic pathway
Statement III is Incorrect: The conversion of Fructose-6-phosphate to Fructose-1, 6-bisphosphate utilizes (consumes) ATP, it does not produce it. Furthermore, this step is generally considered irreversible.

Question:
Assertion (A): In a resting neuron, the axoplasm contains a high concentration of negatively charged proteins that cannot cross the membrane
Reason (R): These proteins are actively transported out of the axoplasm by the sodium-potassium pump
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Reason is False: The negatively charged proteins are large organic molecules that are unable to pass through the membrane; they are not transported out by the Na+-K+ pump

Question:
Consider the following four statements regarding Gibberellins GAs:
Statement 1: The discovery of gibberellins is linked to the foolish seedling disease of wheat seedlings caused by a fungal pathogen Gibberella fujikuroi
Statement 2: Gibberellic acid GA3 was one of the first gibberellins to be discovered and remains the most intensively studied form
Statement 3: Spraying sugarcane crops with gibberellins decreases the length of the stem, thereby concentrating the sugar and increasing the sugar yield
Statement 4: Gibberellins cause fruits like apple to elongate and improve their shape, and they also delay senescence
Which of the following combinations represents the correct statements?
Options: 1, 2 and 3 only
1, 3 and 4 only
2 and 4 only
All 1, 2, 3 and 4
Solution:
Statement 1 is incorrect: The discovery of gibberellins is linked to the foolish seedling disease of rice not wheat seedlings caused by a fungal pathogen Gibberella fujikuroi
Statement 3 is Incorrect: Spraying sugarcane with gibberellins increases the length of the stem (by up to 20 tonnes per acre), rather than decreasing it.

Question:

Statement IV: Fats are absorbed through lymph in the lacteals present in the gastric villi

I, III and IV
II and IV only
I and III only
Solution:
Statement II is Incorrect: The mineral distribution of tissue fluid is similar to that of plasma, not different

Question:

Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is incorrect: In artificial hybridisation, emasculation is the removal of the anthers from the flower bud

Question:
Consider the following four statements regarding the Cerebral Cortex:
Statement 1: The cerebral cortex is referred to as the grey matter due to its greyish appearance caused by the concentration of neuron cell bodies
Statement 2: The cortex contains motor areas, sensory areas, and large regions that are clearly motor in function
Statement 3: The large regions called association areas are responsible for complex functions like intersensory associations, memory, and communication
Statement 4: Fibres of the tracts are covered with the myelin sheath, which constitute the inner part of the cerebral hemisphere called white matter
Which of the following combinations is correct?
Options: 1, 2 and 3
1, 3 and 4
2 and 4 only
All 1, 2, 3 and 4
Solution: Statement 2 is Incorrect: The association areas are regions that are neither clearly sensory nor motor in function. The statement incorrectly claims they are "clearly motor"

Question:
Assertion (A): The clavicle is a long slender bone with two curvatures
Reason (R): The scapula is a large triangular flat bone situated in the dorsal part of the thorax between the second and the seventh ribs
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are factually correct descriptions of bones in the pectoral girdle. However, the shape/location of the scapula (Reason) does not explain why the clavicle has two curvatures (Assertion). They are independent facts.

Question:
Read the following statements and choose the correct sequence of True (T) and False (F):
Statement I: In humans, the genetic makeup of the egg determines the sex of the child
Statement II: The Y-chromosome carries factors that trigger the development of male characteristics in humans
Statement III: In birds, the sex of the progeny is determined by the type of sperm that fertilizes the egg
Statement IV: Genetic disorders can be broadly grouped into two categories – Mendelian disorders and Chromosomal disorders
Options: I-T, II-F, III-F, IV-F
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-F
I-F, II-T, III-F, IV-T
I-F, II-F, III-F, IV-T
Solution:
Statement I is false: In humans, it is the sperm (X or Y) that determines the sex, as the egg always carries an X-chromosome.
Statement III is false: In birds (female heterogamety), the egg (Z or W) determines the sex, as the sperm always carries a Z-chromosome.

Question: The given diagram refers to ovum surrounded by few sperms. Identify A, B and C in the diagram:

Solution: The correct matching is: A–Zona pellucida, B–Perivitelline space, C–Corona radiata

Question:

Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A

Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct

Question:
Statement I: In Zygote Intra Fallopian Transfer (ZIFT), the zygote is transferred into the uterus after it reaches the 32-blastomere stage
Statement II: In Gamete Intra Fallopian Transfer (GIFT), an ovum collected from a donor is transferred into the fallopian tube of another female
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is Incorrect: In ZIFT, the zygote or early embryo with up to 8 blastomeres is transferred into the fallopian tube. If it is more than 8 blastomeres, it is transferred into the uterus (IUT).

Question:

II. GM organisms always have predictable effects on ecosystems
III. GEAC approves GM research and GMO safety for public services in India
IV. Patent issues arise from commercial use of indigenous biological resources

Options: Four
One
Two
Three
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: GM organisms do not have predictable effects on ecosystems.

Question:
Read the following statements carefully and select the correct answer among the following
Statement I: Many marine fish are infested with endoparasitic copepods
Statement II: Abingdon tortoise in Galapagos Islands became extinct within a decade after goats were introduced on the island, apparently due to the greater browsing efficiency of the goats
Statement III: Monarch butterfly is highly distasteful to its predator (bird) because of a special chemical present in its body, which it acquires during its caterpillar stage by feeding on a poisonous weed
Statement IV: In accordance with their life styles, parasites evolved special adaptations such as the loss of unnecessary sense organs, presence of adhesive organs or suckers to cling on to the host, loss of high reproductive capacity and efficient, well-developed digestive system
Options: Statements I and III are correct
Statements II and III are correct
Statements II, III and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: Many marine fish are infested with ectoparasitic copepods
Statement IV is incorrect: In accordance with their life styles, parasites evolved special adaptations such as the loss of unnecessary sense organs, presence of adhesive organs or suckers to cling on to the host, loss of digestive system and high reproductive capacity

Question: The great German naturalist and geographer Alexander von Humboldt observed that within a region species richness increased with increasing explored area, but only up to a limit. In fact, relation between species richness and area for a wide variety of taxa (angiosperm plants, birds, bats, freshwater fishes) turns out to be rectangular hyperbola. Now find out correct equations shown in the graph:
Options: I: log S = log C + Z log A; II: S = CA<sup>Z</sup>
I: S =CA<sup>Z</sup>; II: log S = log C + Z log A
I: S = CA<sup>Z</sup> + log C; II: log S = log C + Z log A
I: S =CA<sup>Z</sup> + log A; II: log S = log C + Z log A
Solution: The correct matching is: I: S =CAZ; II: log S = log C + Z log A

Question:
Assertion (A): Biological names are generally taken from Latin and written in italics
Reason (R): Latin is a dead language, and using it ensures that the names remain standardized and do not change over time
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The reason correctly explains why Latin is chosen. Since it is a "dead" language (not in active colloquial use), it is not subject to the evolution and changes that living languages undergo, making it perfect for stable scientific nomenclature.

Question:
Consider the following four statements and select the correct answer:
I. Photosynthesis is responsible for the capturing of oxygen into the atmosphere by green plants.

II. Green plants carry out ‘photosynthesis’, a physico-chemical process by which they use light energy to drive the synthesis of organic compounds.

III. Chlorophyll (green pigment of the leaf), light and CO2 are required for photosynthesis to occur

IV. Photosynthesis occurred only in the green parts of the leaves in the absence of light
Options: I and IV are correct
II and III are correct
I and III are correct
II and IV are correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: Photosynthesis is responsible for the releasing of oxygen into the atmosphere by green plants.
Statement IV is incorrect: Photosynthesis occurred only in the green parts of the leaves in the presence of light

Question:
Assertion (A): The anatomical setup of the lungs in the thorax is such that any change in the volume of the thoracic cavity will be reflected in the lung (pulmonary) cavity
Reason (R): The thoracic chamber is an air-tight chamber formed dorsally by the vertebral column, ventrally by the sternum, and laterally by the ribs
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Reason is True and Correct Explanation: The "air-tight" nature and the rigid boundaries described in the Reason are exactly why the volume changes in the thorax are forced upon the lungs. We cannot alter pulmonary volume directly; we must do it via the thoracic cavity.

Question:
Statement I: Impulse transmission across an electrical synapse is very similar to impulse conduction along a single axon
Statement II: Electrical synapses are the most common type of synapses found in the human neural system
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: Electrical synapses are rare in our system; chemical synapses are the most common.

Question: Match the Animal Pollinator in Column I with the Specific Reward/Interaction in Column II:

A-iv, B-ii, C-iii, D-i
A-i, B-iv, C-ii, D-iii
A-ii, B-iv, C-i, D-iii
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-iv, C-i, D-iii

Question:
Select the correct statements:
Statement I: The Collecting Duct extends from the cortex of the kidney to the inner parts of the medulla
Statement II: The PCT helps to maintain the pH and ionic balance of the body fluids by selective absorption of hydrogen ions and ammonia into the filtrate
Statement III: Large amounts of water could be reabsorbed from the Collecting Duct region to produce a concentrated urine
Statement IV: The ascending limb of Henle's loop allows the passage of small amounts of urea into the medullary interstitium to keep up the osmolarity
Options: I and II only
III and IV only
I and III only
All of the above
Solution:
Statement II is incorrect: The PCT helps to maintain the pH and ionic balance of the body fluids by selective secretion of hydrogen ions and ammonia into the filtrate
Statement IV is Incorrect: It is the Collecting Duct (not the ascending limb) that allows the passage of small amounts of urea into the medullary interstitium. The ascending limb transports NaCl.

Question:
Assertion (A): The reaction rate of the enzyme carbonic anhydrase is extremely high, producing about 600,000 molecules of carbonic acid every second
Reason (R): The enzyme accelerates the reaction rate by about 10 million times compared to the uncatalyzed reaction
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The incredible acceleration factor (R) is the reason why the enzyme is considered to have such a high reaction rate (A).

Question:
Read the following statements:
Statement I: Enzyme activity generally declines both below and above the optimum value of temperature.
Statement II: Enzymes isolated from organisms who normally live under extremely low temperatures (e.g., vents and sulphur springs), are stable and retain their catalytic power even at high temperatures (up to 80°- 90°C)
Statement III: With the increase in substrate concentration, the velocity of the enzymatic reaction rises at first and eventually reaches a maximum velocity (Vmax)
Statement IV: The activity of an enzyme is also sensitive to the presence of specific chemicals that bind to the enzyme; when the binding shuts off enzyme activity, the process is called inhibition.
Select the correct option:
Options: Statements I, II, and III are correct
Statements I, III, and IV are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Only Statement I is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: Enzymes isolated from organisms who normally live under extremely high temperatures (e.g., hot vents and sulphur springs), are stable and retain their catalytic power even at high temperatures (up to 80°-90°C)

Question:
Assertion (A): Malpighian tubules are the primary excretory organs in cockroaches
Reason R: They are present at the junction of the foregut and midgut
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Reason is Incorrect: Malpighian tubules are present at the junction of the midgut and hindgut, not the foregut and midgut (where hepatic caeca are found).

Question:
Statement I: The frog heart is 3-chambered, consisting of two atria and one ventricle
Statement II: A triangular structure called the sinus venosus joins the left atrium and receives blood through the major veins called vena cava
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: The sinus venosus joins the right atrium (not the left) on the dorsal side.

Question:
Consider the following four statements and select the correct statements among the following:
Statement I: Frogs are poikilotherms (cold-blooded), meaning their body temperature varies with the temperature of the environment
Statement II: They have the ability to change the colour of their skin to hide from their enemies (camouflage), a protective coloration called mimicry
Statement III: During summer and winter, they undergo aestivation and hibernation, respectively, to protect themselves from extreme temperatures.
Statement IV: The body of a frog is divisible into head, neck, and trunk, and a tail is absent
Options: Statements I, II and IV are correct
Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements II and III are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: The body of a frog is divisible into head and trunk only. A neck and tail are absent.

Question:
Read the following four statements regarding taxonomic categories and choose the correct sequence of True (T) and False (F):
I. As we go higher from species to kingdom, the number of common characteristics goes on increasing
II. Order is a higher category which is the assemblage of families which exhibit a few similar characters
III. Families are characterised on the basis of both vegetative and reproductive features of plant species
IV. Classes comprising animals like fishes, amphibians, reptiles, birds, and mammals constitute the next higher category called Division
Options: I-T, II-F, III-F, IV-F
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-F
I-F, II-F, III-T, IV-T
I-F, II-T, III-T, IV-F
Solution:
Statement 1 is False: As we go higher (Species → Kingdom), the number of common characteristics decreases. Lower taxa share more specific characteristics.
Statement 4 is False: For animals, this category is called Phylum. The term 'Division' is used for plants.

Question:
Statement I: Megasporogenesis is the process of formation of megaspores from the Megaspore Mother Cell (MMC) through mitotic divisions
Statement II: The MMC is a large cell containing dense cytoplasm and a prominent nucleus
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is Incorrect: Megasporogenesis involves meiotic division, not mitotic. One diploid MMC produces four haploid megaspores.

Question:
Statement I: Euglenoids have two flagella, a short and a long one
Statement II: Their pigments are identical to those present in higher plants
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct

Question:
Assertion (A): The event of heterospory in genera like Selaginella and Salvinia is considered a precursor to the seed habit
Reason (R): In these plants, the female gametophytes are retained on the parent sporophytes for variable periods, allowing the development of the zygote into a young embryo within the gametophyte
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The Reason correctly explains why this is a precursor to the seed habit: the retention of the female gametophyte acts similarly to the retention of the embryo sac in seed-bearing plants, providing a protected environment for the embryo.

Question:
Statement I: Underproduction of hormones by the adrenal cortex alters carbohydrate metabolism causing acute weakness and fatigue leading to a disease called Addison’s disease
Statement II: The adrenal medulla is often referred to as the 4S gland (Sugar, Salt, Sex, Stress).
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: The Adrenal Cortex is typically associated with regulating Sugar (Glucocorticoids), Salt (Mineralocorticoids), and Sex (Androgens). The "4S" mnemonic is often applied to the adrenal glands generally, but specifically, the Medulla is the "3F" gland (Fight, Flight, Fright). In strict NCERT terms, the metabolic regulations listed are functions of the Cortex hormones, making the attribution to the Medulla incorrect.

Question:
Match the Plant in Column I with its specific size/category in Column II:

 

Column I

 

Column II

A

Wolffia

(i)

Gymnosperm with branched stem

B

Eucalyptus

(ii)

Microscopic Angiosperm

C

Ginkgo

(iii)

Living Fossil (Gymnosperm)

D

Cedrus

(iv)

Tall Angiosperm tree (over 100 meters)
Options: A-ii, B-i, C-iii, D-iv
A-i, B-ii, C-iv, D-iii
A-ii, B-iv, C-iii, D-i
A-i, B-iii, C-ii, D-iv
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-iv, C-iii, D-i

Question:
Assertion (A): Health includes physical, mental, and social well-being
Reason (R): In Pneumonia, the alveoli get filled with fluid leading to severe problems in respiration and reduced oxygenation of the blood
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both are true statements. In Pneumonia, the infection by Streptococcus pneumoniae or Haemophilus influenzae causes the alveoli to fill with fluid. This drastic reduction in the respiratory surface area leads to a lack of oxygen in the blood (cyanosis), which manifests as the bluish discoloration of nails and lips.

Question: In the above figure, what is indicated by ‘a’ in the figure?
Options: Bacteriophage
Bacteria
Lichen
Fungus
Solution: The correct matching is: Bacteriophage

Question: Match the growth model in Column I with its characteristic feature in Column II:
Options: A-ii, B-iii, C-iv, D-i
A-iii, B-ii, C-i, D-iv
A-iii, B-i, C-ii, D-iv
A-iv, B-ii, C-iii, D-i
Solution: The correct matching is: A-iii, B-ii, C-i, D-iv

Question:
After studying the following statements select the correct answer among the following
Statement I: Decomposition is largely an oxygen-requiring process
Statement II: Fragmentation is the process where detritivores (like earthworms) break down detritus into smaller particles
Statement III: By the process of leaching, water-soluble organic nutrients go down into the soil horizon and get precipitated as unavailable salts
Statement IV: Bacterial and fungal enzymes degrade detritus into simpler inorganic substances; this process is called humification
Options: Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Statements I and II are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement III is incorrect: By the process of leaching, water-soluble inorganic nutrients go down into the soil horizon and get precipitated as unavailable salts
Statement IV is incorrect: The process described (enzymatic degradation into simpler inorganic substances) is called catabolism. Humification leads to the accumulation of a dark-coloured amorphous substance called humus.

Question:
Read the following statements carefully and select the correct answer among the following
Statement I: Decomposers break down complex organic matter into inorganic substances like carbon dioxide, water and nutrients and the process is called decomposition
Statement II: Humus is further degraded by some microbes and release of inorganic nutrients occurs by the process of mineralization
Statement III: In a pond ecosystem, the autotrophic components include the zooplankton, some algae and the floating, submerged and marginal plants found at the edges
Statement IV: Primary productivity depends on the plant species inhabiting a particular area
Options: Statements I and II only are correct
Statements III and IV are correct
Statements I, II, and III are correct
All statements are correct except III
Solution: Statement III is incorrect: In a pond ecosystem, the autotrophic components include the phytoplankton, some algae and the floating, submerged and marginal plants found at the edges

Question:
Statement I: The first meiotic division of a primary oocyte is completed within the tertiary follicle
Statement II: The first meiotic division is an equal division resulting in the formation of two haploid cells of similar size
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: The division is unequal, resulting in a large haploid secondary oocyte and a tiny first polar body.

Question:
Assertion (A): During Diakinesis, the chromosomes are fully condensed and the meiotic spindle is assembled
Reason (R): Diakinesis represents the transition to Metaphase I
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
Solution: Both are true descriptions of the stage. However, R is more of a temporal definition (when it happens) rather than a causal explanation of the events in A (why condensation completes or spindle assembles).

Question:
Read the following statements:
Statement I: The protein portion of the enzyme is called the apoenzyme.
Statement II: Prosthetic groups are organic compounds that are tightly bound to the apoenzyme; for example, in peroxidase, haem is the prosthetic group.
Statement III: Co-enzymes are organic compounds whose association with the apoenzyme is permanent and irreversible
Statement IV: Coenzyme nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (NAD) and NADP contain the vitamin riboflavin
Select the correct option:
Options: Statements I and III are correct
Statements III and IV are incorrect
Statements II and III are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement III is Incorrect: Co-enzymes are usually transiently associated with the apoenzyme, often occurring mainly during catalysis (e.g., NAD, NADP).
Statement IV is incorrect: Coenzyme nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (NAD) and NADP contain the vitamin niacin.

Question:
Assertion (A): Materials to be packaged in the form of vesicles from the ER fuse with the trans face of the Golgi apparatus
Reason (R): Materials move from the cis face to the trans face within the Golgi cisternae for modification and packaging
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution:
Assertion is False: Materials from the ER fuse with the cis (forming) face, not the trans face.
Reason is True: The direction of flow for processing is indeed from the cis (convex) face to the trans (concave) face, where they are eventually released.

Question:
Statement I: Sponges exhibit the tissue level of organisation and are bilaterally symmetrical
Statement II: Platyhelminthes exhibit the organ level of organisation
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is Incorrect: Sponges exhibit the cellular level of organisation and are mostly asymmetrical.

Question:
Assertion (A): Lactational amenorrhea is a highly reliable method of contraception for up to two years of breastfeeding
Reason (R): Intense lactation prevents the secretion of gonadotropins, thereby inhibiting ovulation and the menstrual cycle
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is False: According to NCERT, this method is reported to be effective only up to a maximum period of six months following parturition.

Question:
Read the two statements and choose the correct option:
Statement I: The exoskeleton of arthropods consists of a complex polysaccharide called chitin
Statement II: Complex polysaccharides found in nature, such as chitin, are mostly heteropolymers
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: Complex polysaccharides like Chitin are mostly homopolymers not heteropolymers. It is a homopolysaccharide of NAG.

Match the specialized connective tissue in Column I with the properties of its matrix in Column II:

BBone(ii)Solid and pliable, resists compression

A-ii, B-iv, C-i, D-iii
A-iii, B-i, C-iv, D-ii
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-i, C-iv, D-iii

Question: Match the bioactive molecule in Column I with its microbial source in Column II:
Options: A-ii, B-iv, C-i, D-iii
A-i, B-ii, C-iii, D-iv
A-iv, B-iii, C-ii, D-i
A-iii, B-i, C-iv, D-ii
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-iv, C-i, D-iii

Question: Choose the correct option from A, B and C:
Options: A–Tubectomy, B–Implants, C–Copper-T
A–Condoms, B–Copper-T, C–Implants
A–Vasectomy, B–Condoms, C–Copper-T
A–Copper-T, B–Condoms, C–Implants
Solution: The correct matching is: A–Condoms, B–Copper-T, C–Implants

Question:
Read the following statements and choose the correct sequence of True (T) and False (F):
Statement I: DNA is a negatively charged molecule
Statement II: Treatment with divalent cations like Ca2+ makes the cell wall of bacteria more permeable to DNA
Statement III: Chitinase is used to break open the cells of dicot plants during DNA isolation
Statement IV: The Ti-plasmid of Agrobacterium is naturally found in both dicots and monocots
Options: I-T, II-F, III-F, IV-F
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-F
I-F, II-F, III-T, IV-T
I-F, II-T, III-F, IV-T
Solution:
Statement III is False: Chitinase is used for fungi; cellulase is used for plants.
Statement IV is False: Agrobacterium tumefaciens is a pathogen specifically of several dicot plants.

Question:
Assertion (A): Benign tumors normally remain confined to their original location and do not spread to other parts of the body
Reason (R): Cells sloughed from such benign tumors, reach distant sites through the blood and start a new tumor wherever they get lodged
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Reason is false: Benign tumors normally remain confined to their original location and do not spread to other parts of the body and cause little damage. The malignant tumors, on the other hand are a mass of proliferating cells called neoplastic or tumor cells. Cells sloughed from such malignant tumors reach distant sites through blood, and wherever they get lodged in the body, they start a new tumor there. This property called metastasis is the most feared property of malignant tumors

Question:
Match the tumor type/property in Column I with its definition in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Benign Tumor(i)Most feared property of malignant tumors
B.Malignant Tumor(ii)Remain confined to their original location
C.Metastasis(iii)Mass of proliferating neoplastic cells
D.Contact Inhibition(iv)Property lost by cancer cells
Options: A-ii, B-iii, C-i, D-iv
A-iii, B-ii, C-iv, D-i
A-ii, B-iv, C-i, D-iii
A-iv, B-i, C-ii, D-iii
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-iii, C-i, D-iv

Question:
Assertion (A): Sacred groves in Meghalaya are last refuges for a large number of rare and threatened plants
Reason (R): Sacred groves are examples of ex situ conservation where plants are taken out of their natural habitat to be protected
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Reason is false: Sacred groves are a form of in situ (on-site) conservation, as the species are protected within their natural environment through traditional beliefs
